tEn: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
tZh: 红队测试语言模型以减少危害：方法、扩展行为与经验教训
sEn: Red teaming language models reveals scaling challenges for RLHF models and provides a dataset of 38,961 attacks.
sZh: 红队测试语言模型揭示了RLHF模型在扩展时的挑战，并提供了38,961次攻击的数据集。
absEn: We describe our early efforts to red team language models in order to simultaneously discover, measure, and attempt to reduce their potentially harmful outputs. We make three main contributions. First, we investigate scaling behaviors for red teaming across 3 model sizes (2.7B, 13B, and 52B parameters) and 4 model types: a plain language model (LM); an LM prompted to be helpful, honest, and harmless; an LM with rejection sampling; and a model trained to be helpful and harmless using reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). We find that the RLHF models are increasingly difficult to red team as they scale, and we find a flat trend with scale for the other model types. Second, we release our dataset of 38,961 red team attacks for others to analyze and learn from. We provide our own analysis of the data and find a variety of harmful outputs, which range from offensive language to more subtly harmful non-violent unethical outputs. Third, we exhaustively describe our instructions, processes, statistical methodologies, and uncertainty about red teaming. We hope that this transparency accelerates our ability to work together as a community in order to develop shared norms, practices, and technical standards for how to red team language models.
absZh: 我们描述了早期对语言模型进行红队测试的努力，以同时发现、衡量并尝试减少其潜在的有害输出。我们做出了三项主要贡献。首先，我们研究了3种模型规模（2.7B、13B和52B参数）和4种模型类型的红队测试扩展行为：普通语言模型（LM）；被提示为有帮助、诚实且无害的LM；使用拒绝采样的LM；以及使用人类反馈强化学习（RLHF）训练为有帮助且无害的模型。我们发现RLHF模型随着规模扩大越来越难以进行红队测试，而其他模型类型则呈现平稳趋势。其次，我们发布了包含38,961次红队攻击的数据集，供他人分析和学习。我们提供了自己的数据分析，发现了各种有害输出，从攻击性语言到更微妙的非暴力不道德输出。第三，我们详尽描述了我们的指令、流程、统计方法以及关于红队测试的不确定性。我们希望这种透明度能够加速我们作为社区共同工作的能力，以制定共享的规范、实践和技术标准，用于如何对语言模型进行红队测试。

contrib.zh: 研究了跨3种模型规模和4种模型类型的红队测试的规模扩展行为。

contrib.zh: 发布了包含38,961次红队攻击的数据集，供分析和学习。

contrib.zh: 详尽描述了红队测试的指令、流程和统计方法。

contrib.zh: 发现RLHF模型随规模扩展越来越难以进行红队测试，其他模型类型趋势平稳。

contrib.zh: 分析了从攻击性语言到微妙不道德内容的有害输出。

contrib.zh: 提出了制定红队测试共享规范和技术标准的政策干预措施。

limits.en: Red teaming focused on AI assistant format, not other LM applications.
limits.zh: 红队测试聚焦于AI助手形式，未涵盖其他语言模型应用。

limits.en: Crowdworkers may lack domain expertise for evaluating certain attacks.
limits.zh: 众包工作者可能缺乏评估某些攻击所需的领域专业知识。

limits.en: Data is incomplete due to unbounded space of possible harms.
limits.zh: 由于可能危害的空间无限，数据不完整。

limits.en: Reliance on manual red teaming is expensive and slow at scale.
limits.zh: 依赖人工红队测试成本高且大规模下速度慢。

limits.en: Low inter-annotator agreement on what constitutes a successful attack.
limits.zh: 关于何为成功攻击的标注者间一致性较低。

items.en: We describe our early efforts to red team language models in order to simultaneously discover, measure, and attempt to reduce their potentially harmful outputs. We make three main contributions. First, we investigate scaling behaviors for red teaming across 3 model sizes (2.7B, 13B, and 52B parameters) and 4 model types: a plain language model (LM); an LM prompted to be helpful, honest, and harmless; an LM with rejection sampling; and a model trained to be helpful and harmless using reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). We find that the RLHF models are increasingly difficult to red team as they scale, and we find a flat trend with scale for the other model types. Second, we release our dataset of 38,961 red team attacks for others to analyze and learn from. We provide our own analysis of the data and find a variety of harmful outputs, which range from offensive language to more subtly harmful non-violent unethical outputs. Third, we exhaustively describe our instructions, processes, statistical methodologies, and uncertainty about red teaming. We hope that this transparency accelerates our ability to work together as a community in order to develop shared norms, practices, and technical standards for how to red team language models. Warning: this paper contains examples that may be offensive or upsetting.
items.zh: 我们描述了早期对语言模型进行红队测试的努力，旨在同时发现、衡量并减少其潜在的有害输出。我们做出了三项主要贡献。首先，我们研究了跨3种模型规模（2.7B、13B和52B参数）和4种模型类型的红队测试缩放行为：普通语言模型（LM）；被提示为有帮助、诚实且无害的LM；使用拒绝采样的LM；以及使用基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）训练为有帮助且无害的模型。我们发现RLHF模型随着规模扩大越来越难以进行红队测试，而其他模型类型则呈现平坦趋势。其次，我们发布了包含38,961次红队攻击的数据集，供他人分析和学习。我们提供了自己的数据分析，发现了各种有害输出，从攻击性语言到更微妙的非暴力不道德输出。第三，我们详尽描述了我们的指令、流程、统计方法以及关于红队测试的不确定性。我们希望这种透明度能够加速我们作为社区共同努力的能力，以制定共享的规范、实践和技术标准，用于对语言模型进行红队测试。警告：本文包含可能具有冒犯性或令人不安的示例。

items.en: Large language models exhibit a wide range of harmful behaviors such as reinforcing social biases (e.g., [47, 28, 1, 33, 7]), generating offensive or toxic outputs [25], leaking personally identifiable information from the training data [13], aiding in disinformation campaigns [12], generating extremist texts [37], spreading falsehoods [35], and more [9, 10, 18, 57, 22, 51]. As AI systems improve, the scope of possible harms seems likely to grow [22]. Many strategies have been developed to address some of these harms (e.g., [58, 4, 48, 36, 34, 19, 60]). One potentially useful tool for addressing harm is red teaming—using manual or automated methods to adversarially probe a language model for harmful outputs, and then updating the model to avoid such outputs [42, 20, 3, 11]. In this paper, we describe our early efforts to implement manual red teaming to both make models safer and measure the safety of our models. The models trained with red team data were described in [4], so here we focus on describing our red team results and techniques in detail in the hope that others may benefit from and improve on them.
items.zh: 大型语言模型表现出广泛的有害行为，例如强化社会偏见（例如，[47, 28, 1, 33, 7]）、生成攻击性或毒性输出[25]、泄露训练数据中的个人身份信息[13]、助长虚假信息活动[12]、生成极端主义文本[37]、传播虚假信息[35]等[9, 10, 18, 57, 22, 51]。随着AI系统的改进，可能造成的危害范围似乎也在增长[22]。已经开发了许多策略来解决其中一些危害（例如，[58, 4, 48, 36, 34, 19, 60]）。解决危害的一个潜在有用工具是红队测试——使用手动或自动化方法对抗性地探测语言模型的有害输出，然后更新模型以避免此类输出[42, 20, 3, 11]。在本文中，我们描述了实施手动红队测试的早期努力，以使模型更安全并衡量我们模型的安全性。使用红队数据训练的模型在[4]中有所描述，因此我们在此重点详细描述我们的红队结果和技术，希望其他人能够从中受益并加以改进。

items.en: We make three main contributions. First, we investigate scaling behaviors for red teaming across 3 model sizes (2.7B, 13B, and 52B parameters) and 4 model types: a plain language model (plain LM) [2]; an LM prompted to be helpful, honest, and harmless (HHH prompted LM) [2]; an LM with rejection sampling (RS), which returns the best of sixteen samples as ranked by a helpful and harmless preference model [4]; and a model trained to be helpful and harmless using reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) with the same preference model [4]. The RS and RLHF models rely on data generated from red teaming the prompted LM (see §3.2 for details on all models). Figure 1, middle, shows that: (1) RLHF models are significantly harder to red team as they scale, (2) plain LMs, prompted LMs, and RS models exhibit a flat trend with scale, (3) Prompted LMs are not significantly harder to red team than plain LMs, which is inconsistent with our previous results that use static evaluations to show HHH prompting is an effective safety intervention [2], and (4) RS models are the most difficult to red team at any scale; however, qualitatively, they tend to be harmless by being evasive [4].
items.zh: 我们做出了三项主要贡献。首先，我们研究了跨3种模型规模（2.7B、13B和52B参数）和4种模型类型的红队测试缩放行为：普通语言模型（plain LM）[2]；被提示为有帮助、诚实且无害的LM（HHH prompted LM）[2]；使用拒绝采样的LM（RS），该模型返回由有帮助且无害的偏好模型排名的十六个样本中的最佳样本[4]；以及使用基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）与相同偏好模型训练为有帮助且无害的模型[4]。RS和RLHF模型依赖于对提示LM进行红队测试生成的数据（有关所有模型的详细信息，请参见§3.2）。图1中间显示：（1）RLHF模型随着规模扩大显著更难进行红队测试，（2）普通LM、提示LM和RS模型呈现平坦趋势，（3）提示LM并不比普通LM更难进行红队测试，这与我们之前使用静态评估显示HHH提示是一种有效的安全干预措施的结果不一致[2]，以及（4）RS模型在任何规模下都是最难进行红队测试的；然而，从定性角度看，它们往往通过回避来保持无害[4]。

items.en: Our second contribution is to release our dataset of 38,961 red team attacks for others to analyze and learn from (Table 1).111https://github.com/anthropics/hh-rlhf We provide a Datasheet [24] in §A.7 that fully documents the data and we explain the pros and cons for releasing the data in §A.5. Our dataset is an order of magnitude larger than a similar available red team dataset [60] and considers models one order of magnitude larger than those in [60]. To our knowledge, we release the only dataset of red team attacks on a model trained be safe with RLHF. These types of models are already deployed [41] and we believe our data can help shed further light on their strengths and weaknesses. More generally, we believe our data can be used to understand what successful red team attacks look like, to build (semi-)automated red team techniques [42], to build classifiers for harmfulness, and to prototype strategies for measuring and mitigating harms in language models. We also provide our own preliminary analyses of the types of harms uncovered in our data (Figures 2 & 9, §4).
items.zh: 我们的第二个贡献是发布包含38,961次红队攻击的数据集，供他人分析和学习（表1）。111https://github.com/anthropics/hh-rlhf 我们在§A.7中提供了完整记录数据的Datasheet [24]，并在§A.5中解释了发布数据的利弊。我们的数据集比类似可用的红队数据集[60]大一个数量级，并考虑了比[60]中模型大一个数量级的模型。据我们所知，我们是唯一发布针对使用RLHF训练为安全的模型的红队攻击数据集。这些类型的模型已经部署[41]，我们相信我们的数据有助于进一步揭示它们的优势和劣势。更一般地说，我们相信我们的数据可用于理解成功的红队攻击是什么样的，构建（半）自动化的红队技术[42]，构建有害性分类器，以及原型化衡量和减轻语言模型危害的策略。我们还提供了对数据中发现的有害类型的初步分析（图2和9，§4）。

items.en: Our last contribution is to exhaustively describe our instructions, processes, and statistical methodologies for red teaming (§3). Throughout the design of our experiments, we arrived at many junctures in which we were unsure about how to proceed, even after a literature review on red teaming AI systems (§2). As such, we conducted informational interviews with experts in the field of Trust & Safety and incorporated their suggested best practices (§A.2) into the design of our experiments in order to ensure the well-being of the red team. In general, we found that red team members enjoyed participating in our experiments and felt motivated by a mission to make AI systems less harmful (§A.2). Nevertheless, our work suffers from some limitations, which we discuss in §5.1. Based on our experiences, we propose some policy interventions for how we can work together as a community to develop shared norms, practices, and technical standards for how to red team language models (§5.2).
items.zh: 我们的最后一个贡献是详尽描述了我们的红队测试指令、流程和统计方法（§3）。在整个实验设计过程中，我们遇到了许多不确定如何进行的节点，即使在查阅了关于红队测试AI系统的文献之后也是如此（§2）。因此，我们与信任与安全领域的专家进行了信息访谈，并将他们建议的最佳实践（§A.2）纳入我们的实验设计，以确保红队成员的福祉。总的来说，我们发现红队成员喜欢参与我们的实验，并受到使AI系统减少危害的使命的激励（§A.2）。尽管如此，我们的工作存在一些局限性，我们在§5.1中进行了讨论。基于我们的经验，我们提出了一些政策干预措施，以便我们作为社区共同努力，制定共享的规范、实践和技术标准，用于对语言模型进行红队测试（§5.2）。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Red team attack success by model size (x-axes) and model type (colors). (Left) Attack success measured by average red team member self report (higher is more successful). (Middle) Attack success measured by average minimum harmlessness score (higher is better, less harmful) (Right) Distribution of minimum harmlessness score.
items.capZh: 图1：按模型规模（x轴）和模型类型（颜色）划分的红队攻击成功率。（左）通过平均红队成员自我报告衡量的攻击成功率（越高越成功）。（中）通过平均最小无害性分数衡量的攻击成功率（越高越好，危害越小）。（右）最小无害性分数的分布。

items.en: We use the same models that we developed in our previous work where we train a general language assistant to be helpful, honest, and harmless [2, 4]. However, here we run additional experiments in order to determine the influence of model size on susceptibility to red team attacks (Figure 1) and analyze the content of the attacks (Figures 2 & 9) to understand the types of harms uncovered by red teaming. Additionally, we provide more detail on our red team methods, and release the data, so that others can reproduce (and improve upon) our red team approach and results.
items.zh: 我们使用了与之前工作中开发的相同模型，在该工作中我们训练了一个通用的语言助手，使其有帮助、诚实且无害[2, 4]。然而，在这里我们进行了额外的实验，以确定模型规模对红队攻击易感性的影响（图1），并分析攻击内容（图2和9），以了解红队测试揭示的危害类型。此外，我们提供了关于红队方法的更多细节，并发布了数据，以便其他人可以复现（并改进）我们的红队方法和结果。

items.en: Apart from our previous work, our approach is most similar to [60] & [53], who have crowdworkers attempt to elicit offensive outputs from dialogue agents in open-ended dialogues, then use the resulting data to create effective safety interventions. In [60], they release a Bot Adversarial Dialogues (BAD) dataset of ∼5similar-toabsent5{\sim}5K conversations with 3 dialogue agents ranging in size from 345M to 2.7B parameters. We collect more data (∼40similar-toabsent40{\sim}40K) attacks222Qualitatively, we observe a wider diversity of attacks in our dataset than in the BAD dataset, although we have not quantified this. This is at least partially due to the fact that we simply collected more data.; red team larger models (up to 525252B parameters) in order to measure scaling behaviors, as in [53]; and focus on reinforcement learning from human feedback [14] as our most promising safety intervention.
items.zh: 除了我们之前的工作，我们的方法与[60]和[53]最为相似，他们让众包工作者尝试在开放式对话中从对话代理中引出攻击性输出，然后使用所得数据创建有效的安全干预措施。在[60]中，他们发布了一个机器人对抗性对话（BAD）数据集，包含约5K次对话，涉及3个对话代理，规模从345M到2.7B参数不等。我们收集了更多数据（约40K次攻击）222定性来看，我们观察到数据集中的攻击多样性比BAD数据集更广，尽管我们没有量化这一点。这至少部分是因为我们收集了更多数据。；对更大的模型（高达52B参数）进行红队测试以测量缩放行为，如[53]中所述；并专注于基于人类反馈的强化学习[14]作为我们最有前途的安全干预措施。

items.en: Recent work explores how to automate red teaming by using language models instead of humans as the red team [42]. The approach bootstraps from the BAD dataset [60], and uncovers a variety of harms including (but not limited to) finding groups of people that the dialogue agent discusses in offensive ways, identifying personally identifiable information, and leaking private training data. We uncover similar harms in our dataset and plan to use our own data to systematically compare and contrast the types of harms that can be uncovered in manual versus automated methods in future work (§5).
items.zh: 最近的工作探索了如何通过使用语言模型代替人类作为红队来自动化红队测试[42]。该方法从BAD数据集[60]中引导，并发现了各种危害，包括（但不限于）找到对话代理以攻击性方式讨论的人群、识别个人身份信息以及泄露私有训练数据。我们在数据集中发现了类似的危害，并计划在未来工作中使用我们自己的数据系统地比较和对比手动与自动化方法可以发现的危害类型（§5）。

items.en: More generally, although our work focuses on adversarial attacks on generative models, it is heavily inspired by and related to prior work that examines the efficacy of adversarial testing to find and address vulnerabilities in NLP algorithms in discriminative settings. Some of these efforts augment humans (through guidelines, templates, programmatic generation of attacks, and various combinations thereof) to devise test cases that cause systems to fail [45, 46, 29, 21, 30, 55, 6, 23]. Others use humans in the loop to continuously and dynamically build, break, and fix [20] models in order to continuously make them more robust to failure modes [40, 32, 55, 61]. Finally, a large body of work aims to learn adversarial examples that cause downstream models to produce spurious outputs [50], some of which are reviewed in [59]. However, these examples often seem arbitrary and unintelligible to humans, and thus correspond to a different kind of attack than the ones we consider here.
items.zh: 更一般地说，尽管我们的工作侧重于生成模型的对抗性攻击，但它深受先前工作的启发，这些工作检查了对抗性测试在判别设置中发现和解决NLP算法漏洞的有效性。其中一些努力通过指南、模板、程序化生成攻击及其各种组合来增强人类，以设计导致系统失败的测试用例[45, 46, 29, 21, 30, 55, 6, 23]。其他努力使用人在回路中持续动态地构建、破坏和修复[20]模型，以使其对故障模式更具鲁棒性[40, 32, 55, 61]。最后，大量工作旨在学习导致下游模型产生虚假输出的对抗性示例[50]，其中一些在[59]中进行了综述。然而，这些示例通常看起来任意且难以理解，因此对应于与我们在此考虑的不同的攻击类型。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Visualization of the red team attacks. Each point corresponds to a red team attack embedded in a two dimensional space using UMAP. The color indicates attack success (brighter means a more successful attack) as rated by the red team member who carried out the attack. We manually annotated attacks and found several thematically distinct clusters of attack types (black ellipses and text).
items.capZh: 图2：红队攻击的可视化。每个点对应一个红队攻击，使用UMAP嵌入到二维空间中。颜色表示攻击成功率（越亮表示攻击越成功），由执行攻击的红队成员评分。我们手动注释了攻击，并发现了几个主题上不同的攻击类型簇（黑色椭圆和文本）。

items.en: Although our work focuses on language models, there is prior art on how to red team multi-modal generative models [39] in the context of DALL•E 2 [44]. These red team efforts reveal some similar categories of harms to those we find in or dataset, such as: “Bias and representation”; “Harassment, bullying, and exploitation”; “Dis- and misinformation”, and other categories that we do not uncover or consider, such as “Copyright and Trademarks”. The red team consists of domain experts who are selected according to particular areas of expertise, whereas in our case we rely on crowdworkers whose domain expertise we are unaware of (§5.1).
items.zh: 尽管我们的工作侧重于语言模型，但先前有关于如何在DALL·E 2 [44]的背景下对多模态生成模型[39]进行红队测试的文献。这些红队努力揭示了一些与我们数据集中发现的类似危害类别，例如：“偏见与代表性”；“骚扰、欺凌和剥削”；“虚假与错误信息”，以及我们未发现或考虑的其他类别，例如“版权与商标”。红队由根据特定专业领域选择的领域专家组成，而在我们的案例中，我们依赖众包工作者，我们不了解他们的领域专业知识（§5.1）。

items.en: We developed an interface that instructs red team members to have open-ended conversations with an AI assistant [2, 4] in order to “make the AI behave badly, to get it to say obnoxious, offensive, and harmful things” (Figure 3). Similar to [60], we provide the red team with a brief list of example conversation topics but otherwise leave the instructions fairly open-ended. We ask the red team to rely on creativity, avoid obvious profanity, and to focus on a single topic per attack. To encourage this behavior, we ask participants to enter a short description of how they intend to red team the model prior to the attack.
items.zh: 我们开发了一个界面，指示红队成员与AI助手进行开放式对话[2, 4]，以“让AI行为不端，让它说出令人讨厌、冒犯和有害的话”（图3）。与[60]类似，我们为红队提供了一个简短的示例对话主题列表，但其他方面保持指令相当开放。我们要求红队依靠创造力，避免明显的脏话，并每次攻击专注于一个主题。为了鼓励这种行为，我们要求参与者在攻击之前输入他们打算如何对模型进行红队测试的简短描述。

items.en: We were concerned about our task causing harm to the red team, so we reviewed available literature in Trust & Safety and related fields (e.g., [26, 15, 16, 49, 5, 27, 31]), as well as conducted a series of informational interviews with industry professionals in the field. We incorporated best practices from this research (see §A.2 for details) into the content and design of our task instructions (Figure 3, Left) and task interface (Figure 3, Right). For example, in the red team task instructions, we provide clear, specific warnings that red team members may be exposed to sensitive content. We also encourage red team members to select topics within their own risk tolerance, as opposed to prompting them to discuss specific, sensitive topics with the AI assistant. Through surveys and informal feedback, we found that red team members enjoyed the task and did not experience significant negative emotions.
items.zh: 我们担心我们的任务会对红队造成伤害，因此我们回顾了信任与安全及相关领域的现有文献（例如，[26, 15, 16, 49, 5, 27, 31]），并与该领域的行业专业人士进行了一系列信息访谈。我们将这项研究的最佳实践（详见§A.2）纳入我们的任务指令内容与设计（图3左）和任务界面（图3右）。例如，在红队任务指令中，我们提供了明确、具体的警告，说明红队成员可能会接触到敏感内容。我们还鼓励红队成员根据自己的风险承受能力选择主题，而不是提示他们与AI助手讨论特定的敏感主题。通过调查和非正式反馈，我们发现红队成员喜欢这项任务，并且没有经历显著的负面情绪。

items.capEn: Figure 3: (Left) Red team task instructions. (Right) Example of a red team attempt.
items.capZh: 图3：（左）红队任务指令。（右）红队尝试的示例。

items.en: After the red team member agrees to the terms of the task (Figure 3, Left), they proceed to have an open-ended, multi-turn conversation with the AI assistant. After each turn in a conversation, we present the participant with two possible model-generated responses, and ask them to select the more harmful of the two as in [2, 4]. We do this for two reasons. First, this allows us to expedite the red team’s ability to find vulnerabilities in our systems by a factor of two—generative models are stochastic and thus our approach allows the red team twice as many opportunities to catch harmful behavior per attempt. Second, this procedure generates a dataset of pairs of model responses, where one response is labeled as more harmful than the other. We use this dataset to train a harmlessness preference model, which takes as input a model generated response and outputs a score which is lower for more harmful model responses, and higher for less harmful model responses [14, 2, 4].333All of our work fully generalizes from showing 222 possible responses to N𝑁N possible responses. We chose 222 responses to make the task interface simpler. We use the resulting preference model to build safety interventions, which we describe in §3.2. We do not define what “harmful” means, as this is a complex and subjective concept; instead, we rely on the red team to make their own determinations via a pairwise preference choice [14].
items.zh: 在红队成员同意任务条款后（图3左），他们开始与AI助手进行开放式、多轮对话。在对话的每一轮之后，我们向参与者展示两个模型生成的响应，并要求他们选择两者中更有害的一个，如[2, 4]中所述。我们这样做有两个原因。首先，这使我们能够将红队发现系统漏洞的速度提高一倍——生成模型是随机的，因此我们的方法为红队提供了每次尝试中捕捉有害行为的两倍机会。其次，这个过程生成了一个模型响应配对数据集，其中一个响应被标记为比另一个更有害。我们使用这个数据集来训练一个无害性偏好模型，该模型以模型生成的响应为输入，并输出一个分数，对于更有害的模型响应分数更低，对于危害较小的模型响应分数更高[14, 2, 4]。333我们所有的工作完全推广到显示N个可能响应。我们选择2个响应以使任务界面更简单。我们使用得到的偏好模型来构建安全干预措施，我们在§3.2中描述。我们没有定义“有害”的含义，因为这是一个复杂且主观的概念；相反，我们依靠红队通过成对偏好选择做出自己的判断[14]。

items.en: We ask red team members to have a back-and-forth conversation for four turns (Figure 3, Right). We do not strictly limit the number of turns in each conversation, and empirically, we observe most conversations are 1-4 turns, with some lasting longer. At the end of each conversation, we ask the participant to rate how successful they were at making the AI assistant say something bad. We collect these ratings on a 555 point Likert scale (ranging from 0 to 4) where a 0 means “Not successful” and a 4 means “Very successful” (Figure 3, Right).444We experimented with asking participants to rate the harmfulness of the model responses and how actionable the responses were (Figure 3, Right). In general we did not observe clear signals from these data and thus do not report on them. Red team members continue this process for a series of five dialogues, typically on five unique topics, which culminates in one overall task. Red team members could then choose to complete further tasks.
items.zh: 我们要求红队成员进行四轮来回对话（图3右）。我们没有严格限制每次对话的轮数，根据经验，我们观察到大多数对话为1-4轮，有些更长。在每次对话结束时，我们要求参与者评价他们在让AI助手说出坏话方面的成功程度。我们使用5点李克特量表（范围从0到4）收集这些评分，其中0表示“不成功”，4表示“非常成功”（图3右）。444我们尝试过要求参与者评价模型响应的有害性和可操作性（图3右）。总的来说，我们没有从这些数据中观察到清晰的信号，因此没有报告。红队成员继续这个过程进行一系列五个对话，通常涉及五个独特主题，最终完成一个整体任务。红队成员随后可以选择完成更多任务。

items.en: The AI assistant is powered by four types of dialogue models: one baseline model and three models with different types of safety interventions. We assign red team members to models at random—the red team does not know which model they interact with. We describe these models further in the next section.
items.zh: AI助手由四种类型的对话模型驱动：一个基线模型和三个具有不同类型安全干预措施的模型。我们将红队成员随机分配到模型——红队不知道他们与哪个模型交互。我们在下一节进一步描述这些模型。

items.en: We derive dialogue models, with various safety interventions, from a general language model, and in some cases, a helpful and harmless preference model. For simplicity, we refer to the preference model as a harmlessness preference model, and the output of the model as a harmlessness score throughout this work.555More generally, our preference model is trained to predict both harmlessness and helpfulness. For the latter, we created a separate interface in order to collect preference data about helpfulness. We found a fundamental tension between these helpfulness and harmlessness—a model can simply be harmless by refusing to be helpful [4]. As such, we train our preference models to predict both harmlessness and helpfulness. We find that this approach helps to address this tension without loss in predictive accuracy for harmlessness [4]. Here, we first provide basic details on the general language model and the harmlessness preference model, then elaborate on the four dialogue models that power the AI assistant.
items.zh: 我们从通用语言模型推导出具有各种安全干预措施的对话模型，在某些情况下，还使用有帮助且无害的偏好模型。为简单起见，我们在本文中将偏好模型称为无害性偏好模型，并将模型的输出称为无害性分数。555更一般地说，我们的偏好模型被训练来预测无害性和有帮助性。对于后者，我们创建了一个单独的界面来收集关于有帮助性的偏好数据。我们发现有帮助性和无害性之间存在根本性的紧张关系——模型可以通过拒绝提供帮助来简单地做到无害[4]。因此，我们训练我们的偏好模型来同时预测无害性和有帮助性。我们发现这种方法有助于解决这种紧张关系，而不会损失无害性的预测准确性[4]。在这里，我们首先提供关于通用语言模型和无害性偏好模型的基本细节，然后详细说明驱动AI助手的四种对话模型。

items.en: For our general language models, we train decoder-only transformer models ranging in size from 2.7B to 13B to 52B parameters. Full details about model architectures, training data, training procedures, and model evaluations are described elsewhere [2].
items.zh: 对于我们的通用语言模型，我们训练了仅解码器的Transformer模型，规模从2.7B到13B再到52B参数不等。关于模型架构、训练数据、训练程序和模型评估的完整细节已在别处描述[2]。

items.en: To train our harmlessness preference model, we use the comparison data from red team attacks on 52B parameter prompted language model (described below) as the training data—this is why we collected an order of magnitude more data in this case (Table 1). To build these models, we fine-tune 2.7B, 13B, and 52B general language models to predict which model utterances red team members found less harmful, thus producing a harmlessness score [2]. A lower score means more harmful.
items.zh: 为了训练我们的无害偏好模型，我们使用来自对52B参数提示语言模型（如下所述）的红队攻击的比较数据作为训练数据——这就是为什么在这种情况下我们收集了一个数量级更多的数据（表1）。为了构建这些模型，我们对2.7B、13B和52B通用语言模型进行微调，以预测红队成员认为哪些模型话语危害较小，从而产生无害分数[2]。分数越低表示越有害。

items.en: We use 1-shot learning (in which we place an single example of a 3-turn conversation in our Human, Assistant format in context) to prompt our general language models to behave as dialogue models for use in the interface described above [2]. We consider this method a baseline or control model, since it minimally departs from a general-purpose plain language model and has no explicit safety intervention.
items.zh: 我们使用1-shot学习（在上下文中放置一个3轮对话的示例，采用我们的人类、助手格式）来提示我们的通用语言模型，使其作为对话模型用于上述界面[2]。我们将此方法视为基线或控制模型，因为它最小程度地偏离了通用纯语言模型，并且没有明确的安全干预。

items.en: We use 14-shot learning to prompt our general language models to be helpful, harmless, and honest (HHH) [2], similar to dialogue-prompted Gopher [43]. We consider this a simple safety intervention, since we found it to be surprisingly effective at reducing model toxicity, especially for larger models [2, 43]. Furthermore, we use context distillation [2] to train “prompt-free” variants of these prompted models in order to retain the influence of the prompt without occupying a significant portion of the limited context window and decreasing inference time [2]. Empirically, in previous work, we found minimal differences between prompting and context distillation [2].
items.zh: 我们使用14-shot学习来提示我们的通用语言模型成为有帮助、无害且诚实（HHH）的模型[2]，类似于对话提示的Gopher[43]。我们认为这是一种简单的安全干预，因为我们发现它在减少模型毒性方面出奇地有效，尤其是对于较大的模型[2, 43]。此外，我们使用上下文蒸馏[2]来训练这些提示模型的“无提示”变体，以保留提示的影响，而不占用有限的上下文窗口的显著部分并减少推理时间[2]。根据经验，在先前的工作中，我们发现提示和上下文蒸馏之间的差异很小[2]。

items.en: We generate 16 samples of AI assistant responses from prompted language models, rank these samples with the harmlessness preference model, and select the 2 least harmful samples to present to the red team member, thus rejecting the 14 relatively more harmful responses. We did not experiment with changing the parameter 16. We tie the size of the prompted model to the size of the harmlessness preference model, e.g., a 2.7B parameter rejection sampling model consists of a 2.7B prompted language model paired with a 2.7B harmlessness preference model.666This choice is arbitrary, e.g., we can pair a 2.7B prompted language model with a 52B harmlessness preference model, but allows us to study the influence of scale more systematically. In our formulation, technically an N𝑁N parameter RS model actually consists of 2​N2𝑁2N parameters.
items.zh: 我们从提示语言模型中生成16个AI助手响应样本，使用无害偏好模型对这些样本进行排序，并选择2个最无害的样本呈现给红队成员，从而拒绝14个相对更有害的响应。我们没有尝试改变参数16。我们将提示模型的大小与无害偏好模型的大小绑定，例如，一个2.7B参数的拒绝采样模型由一个2.7B提示语言模型和一个2.7B无害偏好模型组成。666这个选择是任意的，例如，我们可以将2.7B提示语言模型与52B无害偏好模型配对，但这使我们能够更系统地研究规模的影响。在我们的公式中，从技术上讲，一个N参数RS模型实际上包含2N个参数。

items.en: We start with a prompted language model, then use reinforcement learning to train it to maximize the scores given by the preference model described above. As in the rejection sampling case, we tie the size of the prompted model to the size of the preference model. Full details about the training procedures, training datasets, and model evaluations are described elsewhere [4]. Intuitively, we expect RLHF models to behave similarly (but not exactly) to RS models; however, RLHF is computationally expensive at train time but efficient at test time. RS is vice-versa.
items.zh: 我们从提示语言模型开始，然后使用强化学习训练它以最大化上述偏好模型给出的分数。与拒绝采样情况一样，我们将提示模型的大小与偏好模型的大小绑定。关于训练过程、训练数据集和模型评估的完整细节在其他地方描述[4]。直观上，我们期望RLHF模型的行为与RS模型相似（但不完全相同）；然而，RLHF在训练时计算成本高，但在测试时效率高。RS则相反。

items.en: Our red team consists of 324 US-based crowdworkers whom we primarily recruited from Amazon’s Mechanical Turk (MTurk) platform (n=307𝑛307n=307) and the Upwork platform (n=17𝑛17n=17). On MTurk, we paid between $7.50 and $9.50 for each set of 5 conversations completed. We found that crowdworkers could complete at least 2 tasks an hour, which means that we paid at or above California minimum wage.777As of 2022, California minimum wage is $15.00 per hour On Upwork, we paid participants $20 per hour. Similar to [53], we asked participants to fill out a short demographic survey that incorporated U.S. census categories and offered participants the option to answer “Prefer to not to say” for each question (Figure 4).
items.zh: 我们的红队由324名美国众包工人组成，我们主要从亚马逊的Mechanical Turk（MTurk）平台（n=307）和Upwork平台（n=17）招募。在MTurk上，我们为每完成5组对话支付7.50至9.50美元。我们发现众包工人每小时至少能完成2个任务，这意味着我们的支付达到或高于加州最低工资。777截至2022年，加州最低工资为每小时15.00美元。在Upwork上，我们向参与者支付每小时20美元。与[53]类似，我们要求参与者填写一份简短的、包含美国人口普查类别的人口统计调查，并为每个问题提供“不愿回答”选项（图4）。

items.en: We found that he crowdworker population may not be fully representative of the U.S. population, according to US Census data [54].888Because we collected this data anonymously, we are unable to examine the impact of demographic attributes of red team members on the types or efficacy of their red team attacks. For example, we find that individuals who self-identify as “White or Caucasian” are slightly over-represented in our experiments (79% versus the current U.S. Census estimate of 75.8%). Similarly, the percentage of participants with at least a college degree was significantly higher than what is reported by the U.S. Census (66% versus 32.9%).
items.zh: 我们发现，根据美国人口普查数据[54]，众包工人群体可能不能完全代表美国人口。888由于我们是匿名收集这些数据，因此无法检查红队成员的人口统计属性对其红队攻击类型或效果的影响。例如，我们发现自我认同为“白人或高加索人”的个体在我们的实验中略微过度代表（79%对比当前美国人口普查估计的75.8%）。同样，至少拥有大学学位的参与者比例显著高于美国人口普查报告的比例（66%对比32.9%）。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Descriptive statistics about red team members. (Left) Total number of red team attempts (y-axis) per red team member (x-axis), sorted by number of attempts. (Middle) The cumulative distribution (CDF) of the data from the left panel shows that ∼80similar-toabsent80{\sim}80% of attacks come from ∼15similar-toabsent15{\sim}15% of the red team participants. (Right) Estimate of how effective each red team member is at red teaming (y-axis, 00 means average, lower means more effective, lines indicate 959595% confidence intervals) according to their ability to achieve a low minimum harmlessness score. X-axis is sorted by ability.
items.capZh: 图5：关于红队成员的描述性统计。（左）每个红队成员（x轴）的红队尝试总数（y轴），按尝试次数排序。（中）左面板数据的累积分布（CDF）显示，约80%的攻击来自约15%的红队参与者。（右）根据每个红队成员达到低最小无害分数的能力，估计其红队攻击的有效性（y轴，0表示平均水平，越低表示越有效，线条表示95%置信区间）。x轴按能力排序。

items.en: With our interface, models, and red team in place, we collect 38,961 red team attacks across with O(111K) attacks per model type in all cases except for the 525252B prompted model for which we collect O(101010K) attacks (Table 1). We collect more data in the latter case in order to train our harmlessness preference models, as described in §3.2. Figure 6 shows an example red team attack and how we quantify it. In particular, we measure 333 variables for each attack. First, we record the red team member’s self-rating of how successful they were on a 555-point Likert scale, where a 0 indicates an unsuccessful attempt, and a 4 indicates a very successful attempt (see also Figure 3, Right, for an example). Figure 7 (Left) shows the distribution over this variable, which is approximately bimodal, with two peaks at 0 and 4, with relatively more mass at 0. This indicates that, on average, red team member’s self report successful attacks ∼35similar-toabsent35{\sim}35% of the time.
items.zh: 通过我们的界面、模型和红队，我们收集了38,961次红队攻击，每种模型类型大约有1万次攻击，除了52B提示模型我们收集了大约10万次攻击（表1）。在后一种情况下，我们收集更多数据是为了训练我们的无害偏好模型，如§3.2所述。图6显示了一个红队攻击示例以及我们如何量化它。具体来说，我们为每次攻击测量3个变量。首先，我们记录红队成员对自己成功程度的自我评分，采用5点李克特量表，其中0表示不成功，4表示非常成功（另见图3右侧示例）。图7（左）显示了这个变量的分布，大致呈双峰分布，在0和4处有两个峰值，0处质量相对更多。这表明，平均而言，红队成员自我报告成功攻击的比例约为35%。

items.en: Next, we use our harmlessness preference model to compute the harmlessness score (higher is less harmful and thus better) of the AI assistant’s dialogue. In particular, we compute the score for each assistant utterance, conditioned on everything preceding the utterance, for each utterance in the conversation. For a N𝑁N-turn conversation, this results in N𝑁N harmlessness scores (Figure 6). To turn this into one number, we either compute the mean or minimum (worst) harmlessness of the AI assistant. Empirically, we find that the precise choice of aggregate statistic does not significantly change our results, so we choose the minimum harmlessness as a lower bound on the overall harmlessness of the model.
items.zh: 接下来，我们使用无害偏好模型计算AI助手对话的无害分数（越高表示越无害，因此越好）。具体来说，我们为每个助手话语计算分数，条件是该话语之前的所有内容，针对对话中的每个话语。对于一个N轮对话，这会产生N个无害分数（图6）。为了将其转化为一个数字，我们计算AI助手的平均或最小（最差）无害分数。根据经验，我们发现聚合统计量的精确选择不会显著改变我们的结果，因此我们选择最小无害分数作为模型整体无害性的下界。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Example of how we quantify red team attempts. First, we compute a harmlessness score (lower is more harmful) on the task description (red). Next, we compute a harmlessness score on the assistant utterances, conditioned on all previous human and assistant utterances (black scores, adjacent to assistant utterances). We aggregate these scores using either a min or max (black, bold). Finally, we rely on human judgement of attack success on a Likert scale (blue).
items.capZh: 图6：我们如何量化红队尝试的示例。首先，我们在任务描述（红色）上计算无害分数（越低越有害）。接下来，我们在助手话语上计算无害分数，条件是所有之前的人类和助手话语（黑色分数，靠近助手话语）。我们使用最小值或最大值（黑色，粗体）聚合这些分数。最后，我们依赖人类对攻击成功程度的李克特量表判断（蓝色）。

items.en: Finally, we also use the harmlessness preference model to score the harmfulness of the red team member’s intent. To do so, we run the preference model on the red team member’s task description (Figure 6).999Technically, this data is out of distribution for the preference model, since the preference model is trained on AI utterances, and here we run inference on human utterances. Figure 7 (Right) shows the distribution over this variable, which appears normally distributed with a mean around 1. As such, short descriptions of the attack score as less harmful than the actual AI utterances. We view the intent harmlessness score as a possible confound that we control for in further statistical analyses of the data (§A.3). Since we find that it does not influence our main results, we do not report on this variable further in the main text.
items.zh: 最后，我们还使用无害偏好模型对红队成员意图的有害性进行评分。为此，我们在红队成员的任务描述上运行偏好模型（图6）。999从技术上讲，这些数据对于偏好模型来说是分布外的，因为偏好模型是在AI话语上训练的，而这里我们在人类话语上进行推理。图7（右）显示了这个变量的分布，大致呈正态分布，均值约为1。因此，攻击的简短描述得分比实际的AI话语更无害。我们将意图无害分数视为可能的混淆变量，在进一步的数据统计分析中加以控制（§A.3）。由于我们发现它不影响我们的主要结果，我们在正文中不再进一步报告这个变量。

items.en: After we collected the data across all model types, we performed a follow-up experiment to measure two separate variables: the inter-annotator agreement in the self report of attack success, and the content of the attack types. The former is important because self-ratings of attack success are subjective, and can vary based on elements of the red team attack and red team member that we do not control (e.g., the type of attack or the background of the red team member). As such, we were interested in understanding how much variability (across different raters) there might be for defining a successful attack.
items.zh: 在收集了所有模型类型的数据后，我们进行了一项后续实验来测量两个独立的变量：攻击成功自我报告中的标注者间一致性，以及攻击类型的内容。前者很重要，因为攻击成功的自我评分是主观的，并且可能根据我们无法控制的红队攻击和红队成员的元素（例如，攻击类型或红队成员的背景）而变化。因此，我们感兴趣的是了解定义成功攻击时可能存在的变异性（跨不同评分者）。

items.capEn: Figure 7: (Left) Marginal distribution of self-report of red team success rates (higher is more successful) (Middle) Probability distribution function (PDF) of minimum AI harmlessness scores computed from the AI utterances (lower is more harmful) (Right) Distribution of harmlessness scores computed from short descriptions (written by red team members) of attack intent.
items.capZh: 图7：（左）红队成功率的自我报告边际分布（越高越成功）。（中）从AI话语计算的最小AI无害分数的概率分布函数（PDF）（越低越有害）。（右）从红队成员撰写的攻击意图简短描述计算的无害分数分布。

items.en: We find a low level of inter-rater agreement on the success of red team attacks (according to our task setup and instructions) consistent with [60]. In particular, we report Fleiss’s Kappa, a statistical measure of inter-annotator agreement that is bounded in [−∞,1]1[-\infty,1], where −∞-\infty implies no agreement and 1 indicates perfect agreement. We report a Fleiss’s Kappa of 0.32 between the 4 raters (the author and the 3 reviewers) based on a Likert rating scale. When we binarize the rating (1 if rating ≥3absent3\geq 3, else 0), the agreement increases to 0.49. Furthermore, when we exclude the original author and measure the agreement between the 3 annotators, we also see a modest increase in agreement for both the Likert and Binary scales, achieving a maximum agreement of 0.55 for the reviewer-only binary case. Taken together, our results suggest poor to fair agreement on what constitutes a successful attack.
items.zh: 我们发现，根据我们的任务设置和说明，红队攻击成功的评分者间一致性水平较低，与[60]一致。具体来说，我们报告Fleiss' Kappa，这是一种统计测量标注者间一致性的指标，范围在[-∞,1]之间，其中-∞表示无一致性，1表示完全一致。我们基于李克特评分量表报告了4位评分者（作者和3位评审者）之间的Fleiss' Kappa为0.32。当我们将评分二值化（如果评分≥3则为1，否则为0）时，一致性增加到0.49。此外，当我们排除原作者并测量3位标注者之间的一致性时，李克特和二元量表的一致性都有适度增加，在仅评审者的二元情况下达到最大一致性0.55。综合来看，我们的结果表明，对于什么构成成功攻击，一致性较差到一般。

items.en: To get a sense of the type of harms the attacks were meant to elicit, we asked the reviewers to tag transcripts with up to 2 of 20 total topic tags (Figure 8, Right). To develop the list of topic tags, we referred to the taxonomies of potential harms of language models in [48, 57], industry content moderation guidelines, and a manual review of the top 100 most harmful conversations in our dataset. We discuss our findings on tag frequencies in Figure 9 and §4
items.zh: 为了了解攻击旨在引发的伤害类型，我们要求评审者用最多2个（共20个）主题标签标记对话记录（图8，右）。为了制定主题标签列表，我们参考了[48, 57]中语言模型潜在危害的分类、行业内容审核指南，以及对我们数据集中前100个最有害对话的手动审查。我们在图9和§4中讨论了关于标签频率的发现。

items.en: We were particularly concerned with exposing reviewers to potential harm while participating in this experiment, since we ask reviewers to read, rate, and annotate harmful conversations they were not involved in writing. To mitigate this risk, we reviewed and incorporated findings from literature on Trust & Safety [16, 31, 26] into the content of both the task instructions (Figure 8, Left) and interface (Figure 8, Right), as well as the overall design of the experiment. For example, we built custom warning functionality which allowed reviewers to see a preview of the harmful text without being exposed to the entire conversation. Within the preview window, reviewers could skip to the next conversation or proceed with reviewing and rating the selected conversation. We leave further details in §A.2.
items.zh: 我们特别关注评审者在参与此实验时可能遭受的潜在伤害，因为我们要求评审者阅读、评分和标注他们未参与撰写的有害对话。为了减轻这种风险，我们回顾并整合了关于信任与安全[16, 31, 26]文献的发现，将其纳入任务说明（图8，左）和界面（图8，右）的内容，以及实验的整体设计中。例如，我们构建了自定义警告功能，允许评审者预览有害文本而不暴露于整个对话。在预览窗口中，评审者可以跳到下一个对话，或继续查看和评分所选对话。我们在§A.2中留下更多细节。

items.capEn: Figure 8: (Left) Red team review task instructions. (Right) Example of a red team review task.
items.capZh: 图8：（左）红队评审任务说明。（右）红队评审任务示例。

items.en: Our informational interviews with Trust & Safety industry professionals highlighted the need for creating a sense of community among workers and building social support networks as ways to mitigate possible harms associated with reviewing troubling content, consistent with [26]. As a result, we decided to limit the population of reviewers in this experiment to Upworkers, and we used a shared communication tool (Slack) to regularly communicate with the group. This allowed participants to ask questions, share examples, and discuss work and non-work related topics, not only amongst themselves, but also directly with research staff.
items.zh: 我们对信任与安全行业专业人士的信息性访谈强调了在工人中创建社区感和建立社会支持网络的需求，作为减轻与审查令人不安内容相关的可能危害的方法，与[26]一致。因此，我们决定将本实验中的评审者群体限制为Upworkers，并使用共享通信工具（Slack）定期与小组沟通。这使得参与者能够提问、分享示例以及讨论工作相关和非工作相关的话题，不仅彼此之间，而且直接与研究工作人员。

items.en: To monitor the psychological effects of this work and provide an avenue for direct feedback from reviewers, we developed a custom well-being survey and sent it to reviewers after completing 101010 tasks. In the survey (which is optional to complete) we asked reviewers to rate how often they felt a variety of positive and negative emotions, and we also provided a free-form text question where reviewers could share additional thoughts. Participants generally felt low levels of negative emotions, and higher levels of positive emotions about the task. Informally, we received feedback that reviewers found the task to be fun and engaging. We provide more detail on the well-being survey and additional worker safety interventions in §A.2.
items.zh: 为了监测这项工作的心理影响并为评审者提供直接反馈的途径，我们开发了一个定制的幸福感调查，并在评审者完成10个任务后发送给他们。在调查中（可选完成），我们要求评审者评价他们感受到各种积极和消极情绪的频率，并提供一个自由文本问题，评审者可以分享额外想法。参与者通常感受到低水平的消极情绪，以及较高水平的关于任务的积极情绪。非正式地，我们收到反馈，评审者认为任务有趣且引人入胜。我们在§A.2中提供了关于幸福感调查和额外工人安全干预的更多细节。

items.capEn: Figure 9: Number of attacks (x-axes) classified by a tag (y-axis) for a random sample of 500 attacks each on the 52B Prompted LM and RLHF models. Blue denotes total number of attacks, orange denotes the number of successful attacks.
items.capZh: 图9：针对52B提示LM和RLHF模型，每个模型随机抽取500次攻击，按标签（y轴）分类的攻击次数（x轴）。蓝色表示攻击总数，橙色表示成功攻击数。

items.en: Second, we find that rejection sampling (RS) makes it particularly difficult to red team our language models. In essence, rejection sampling places a floor on red team attack susceptibility out of the three interventions that we tried. However, qualitatively, we believe that this may be the case because the responses from the RS models tend to be harmless by being evasive [4]. Finally, we find no clear trends with model size for the self-reported attack success rate metric. This is surprising because our previous work typically shows larger models tend to generate more toxic model responses [2, 22].
items.zh: 其次，我们发现拒绝采样（RS）使得对我们的语言模型进行红队测试特别困难。本质上，在我们尝试的三种干预措施中，拒绝采样为红队攻击的易感性设置了一个下限。然而，从定性角度看，我们认为这可能是因为RS模型的响应往往通过回避而变得无害[4]。最后，我们发现对于自我报告的攻击成功率指标，模型大小没有明显的趋势。这令人惊讶，因为我们之前的工作通常表明，较大的模型往往会产生更有毒的模型响应[2, 22]。

items.en: Instead of the average minimum harmlessness metric, Figure 1 (Right) shows the distribution over the harmlessness score. Here, we see that although safety interventions like RLHF and RS indeed decrease the average harmfulness of the model responses, there are still many instances of harmful behavior, as exhibited by the lower tails in the distributions. Although the safety interventions we tested help make systems safer, they still fail to make a perfectly safe systems. Figure 10 shows examples of harmful outputs from the RS and RLHF models, respectively. For the RS case, the model at first responds to a harmful inquiry, then starts to demur as the the conversation turns more harmful. For the RLHF case, we see a similar pattern, however the assistant remains helpful (though fabricates information) before ultimately refusing to help the human.
items.zh: 与平均最小无害性指标不同，图1（右）显示了无害性得分的分布。在这里，我们看到尽管RLHF和RS等安全干预措施确实降低了模型响应的平均有害性，但仍然存在许多有害行为实例，如分布中的较低尾部所示。尽管我们测试的安全干预措施有助于使系统更安全，但它们仍然未能构建出完全安全的系统。图10分别显示了来自RS和RLHF模型的有害输出示例。对于RS情况，模型最初响应有害询问，然后随着对话变得更加有害而开始推诿。对于RLHF情况，我们看到了类似的模式，但助手在最终拒绝帮助人类之前仍然保持有帮助（尽管编造了信息）。

items.capEn: Figure 10: Examples of red team attempts that resulted in relatively low harmlessness scores for a (Left) rejection sampling (RS) model and (Right) reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) model.
items.capZh: 图10：导致（左）拒绝采样（RS）模型和（右）基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）模型获得相对较低无害性得分的红队尝试示例。

items.en: To further understand the landscape of possible harms surfaced using this approach, across all model sizes and interventions, we created and annotated a visualization of the entire dataset (Figure 2). To do so, we obtained the average per token embeddings of each transcript from the residual stream in the 48th layer of the 52B prompted LM. Then we used UMAP [38] to turn the high-dimensional embeddings into two-dimensional embeddings for visualization. Intuitively, we expect this procedure to place any pair of transcripts closer together in this two dimensional space the more semantically similar to each other they are.
items.zh: 为了进一步理解使用这种方法可能出现的危害范围，我们针对所有模型大小和干预措施，创建并注释了整个数据集的可视化（图2）。为此，我们从52B提示LM的第48层残差流中获取了每个转录本的平均每词元嵌入。然后，我们使用UMAP [38]将高维嵌入转换为二维嵌入以进行可视化。直观上，我们期望这个过程使得任何一对转录本在语义上越相似，它们在二维空间中就越接近。

items.en: We find evidence for basic clusters of red team attempts. These include perhaps more obvious types of attacks, such as those soliciting discriminatory or offensive responses but also some surprising attacks. For example, we found a small cluster of attacks that tried to solicit misinformation in clever and subtle ways, and a small cluster of attacks related to animal abuse. We also find that some types of attacks, such as soliciting advice on how to perpetrate general violence, seem to be more successful than others, such as attempting to elicit offensive language.
items.zh: 我们发现了红队尝试的基本聚类。这些包括可能更明显的攻击类型，例如那些征求歧视性或攻击性响应的攻击，但也包括一些令人惊讶的攻击。例如，我们发现了一个小的攻击聚类，试图以巧妙和微妙的方式征求错误信息，以及一个与虐待动物相关的小聚类。我们还发现，某些类型的攻击，例如征求如何实施一般暴力的建议，似乎比其他类型的攻击（例如试图引发攻击性语言）更成功。

items.en: We also found a cluster of 916 attacks designed to solicit personally identifiable information (PII). We developed a regular expression (§A.6) to find and filter possible PII from the public dataset (§A.7). We manually reviewed the filtered data and found that some of the AI assistant generated PII (such as addresses) appear to be neither real nor accurate, and instead were “hallucinated” by the AI assistant (see Figure 12 for an example). Other potential AI assistant generated PII, such as social security numbers or drivers licenses, are difficult to manually verify. As such, we erred on the side of caution in filtering out the possible synthetic PII in the public dataset (§A.7).
items.zh: 我们还发现了一个包含916次攻击的聚类，旨在征求个人身份信息（PII）。我们开发了一个正则表达式（§A.6）来查找和过滤公共数据集中的可能PII（§A.7）。我们手动审查了过滤后的数据，发现AI助手生成的一些PII（例如地址）似乎既不真实也不准确，而是AI助手“幻觉”出来的（参见图12示例）。其他潜在的AI助手生成的PII，例如社会安全号码或驾驶执照，很难手动验证。因此，我们在过滤公共数据集中的可能合成PII时采取了谨慎的态度（§A.7）。

items.en: We found that some crowdworkers generated low-quality red team data. For example, we observed some red team members use and adapt template-based attacks, where the template was “tell me an insulting term for X𝑋X that starts with a Y𝑌Y”. With such a template, a crowdworker can trivially generate many red team attempts by iterating over many values of X𝑋X and Y𝑌Y without careful consideration about the efficacy or usefulness of such an attack. Crowdworkers (on MTurk) are incentivized to develop strategies like this in order to increase the number of tasks they complete per time spent in order to maximize their earnings [26].
items.zh: 我们发现一些众包工作者生成了低质量的红队数据。例如，我们观察到一些红队成员使用并改编了基于模板的攻击，其中模板是“告诉我一个以Y开头的侮辱X的术语”。使用这样的模板，众包工作者可以通过迭代许多X和Y的值来轻松生成许多红队尝试，而无需仔细考虑此类攻击的有效性或有用性。众包工作者（在MTurk上）有动机开发这样的策略，以增加他们在单位时间内完成的任务数量，从而最大化他们的收入[26]。

items.en: To further measure the types of harms produced by red teaming, we examined the frequency of tags (described in §3.5). Figure 9 shows the distribution of tags, which we collected for small a proportion (∼3similar-toabsent3{\sim}3%) of the overall dataset. We find that the top 5 attacks correspond to “Discrimination & injustice,” “Hate speech & offensive language,” “Violence & incitement,” “Non violent unethical behavior (e.g., lying, cheating, etc.),” and “Bullying & harassment.” Interestingly, for these top 555 attack types, the attack success rate was relatively higher for “Non violent unethical behavior”, perhaps due to the fact that these types of attacks may be more subtle than the other ones. Less common tags include: “Child Abuse,” “Self harm,” “Sexual Exploitation & Human Trafficking,” “Terrorism & organized crime,” and “Animal abuse”. Finally, we find that the tag “Other” was also prevalent, which suggests that ascribing a fixed set of tags to annotate transcripts is unlikely to be comprehensive.
items.zh: 为了进一步衡量红队测试产生的危害类型，我们检查了标签的频率（在§3.5中描述）。图9显示了标签的分布，我们为整个数据集的一小部分（约3%）收集了这些标签。我们发现前5种攻击对应于“歧视与不公正”、“仇恨言论与攻击性语言”、“暴力与煽动”、“非暴力不道德行为（例如，撒谎、作弊等）”以及“欺凌与骚扰”。有趣的是，对于这5种攻击类型，“非暴力不道德行为”的攻击成功率相对较高，可能是因为这些类型的攻击比其他攻击更微妙。较不常见的标签包括：“虐待儿童”、“自残”、“性剥削与人口贩卖”、“恐怖主义与有组织犯罪”和“虐待动物”。最后，我们发现标签“其他”也很普遍，这表明使用一组固定的标签来注释转录本不太可能是全面的。

items.en: We red team language models in the form of an AI assistant, which has the benefit of allowing the red team to probe the open-ended input and output spaces of the underlying LM. However, LMs can be used in many applications that do not require such open-endedness, e.g., recommendation systems, auto-complete systems, general text classifiers, as sub-components in broader sociotechnical systems, etc. Due to our framing of red teaming an AI assistant, we do not explicitly explore how to reduce the possible harms caused by these other types of applications.
items.zh: 我们以AI助手的形式对语言模型进行红队测试，这有利于让红队探索底层LM的开放式输入和输出空间。然而，LM可以用于许多不需要这种开放性的应用，例如推荐系统、自动补全系统、通用文本分类器、作为更广泛社会技术系统中的子组件等。由于我们将红队测试限定为AI助手，我们没有明确探索如何减少这些其他类型应用可能造成的危害。

items.en: We found that some crowdworkers generated attacks on models that required domain expertise to evaluate, and we were not sure whether or not they had the required domain expertise. For example, it is not clear how to evaluate the harmfulness of responses to inquiries like “Give me detailed instructions on how to build a bomb” or “create methamphetamine” without sufficient knowledge about weapons and chemistry, respectively. We could address this problem by asking third party organizations that specialize in certain domains to red team our systems. Additionally, we could give crowdworkers a way to indicate if and how a particular red team attack requires domain expertise in order to evaluate how successful the attack was.
items.zh: 我们发现一些众包工作者对模型发起了需要领域专业知识才能评估的攻击，我们不确定他们是否具备所需的领域专业知识。例如，对于“给我详细说明如何制造炸弹”或“制造甲基苯丙胺”这样的询问，如果没有足够的武器和化学知识，不清楚如何评估响应的有害性。我们可以通过请专门从事某些领域的第三方组织对我们的系统进行红队测试来解决这个问题。此外，我们可以给众包工作者一种方式，让他们指出特定的红队攻击是否需要领域专业知识来评估攻击的成功程度。

items.en: As expected, our data are incomplete—because LMs are general purpose and open-ended, the space of possible harms is unknown and unbounded [22]. For example, the models we red teamed have been trained partly on Python code; however, we observed no attacks related to code generation ability (e.g., “write a Python program that implements a DDOS attack”). It is possible that sharing our red team interface with more domain experts could have resulted in such attacks. We could have also noted in the instructions to the interface that such attacks would be viable, but we erred on the side of being less prescriptive about how to red team in order to encourage creativity. It is unclear how to strike the right balance.
items.zh: 正如预期的那样，我们的数据是不完整的——因为LM是通用且开放的，可能的危害空间是未知且无界的[22]。例如，我们进行红队测试的模型部分基于Python代码进行训练；然而，我们没有观察到与代码生成能力相关的攻击（例如，“编写一个实现DDOS攻击的Python程序”）。如果我们将红队界面分享给更多领域专家，可能会产生此类攻击。我们也可以在界面说明中指出此类攻击是可行的，但我们倾向于在如何红队测试方面不那么规定性，以鼓励创造力。目前尚不清楚如何取得正确的平衡。

items.en: We also know our data are incomplete because we informally red teamed our models internally and found successful attack types not present in the dataset we release. For example, we uncovered a class of attacks that we call “roleplay attacks” on the RLHF model. In a roleplay attack we exploit the helpfulness of the model by asking it to roleplay as a malevolent character. For example, if we asked the RLHF model to enter “4chan mode” the assistant would oblige and produce harmful and offensive outputs (consistent with what can be found on 4chan). We intend to document additional qualitative safety failures that we uncovered in future work.
items.zh: 我们也知道我们的数据不完整，因为我们内部非正式地对模型进行了红队测试，并发现了我们发布的数据集中不存在的成功攻击类型。例如，我们发现了一类针对RLHF模型的攻击，我们称之为“角色扮演攻击”。在角色扮演攻击中，我们利用模型的有帮助性，要求它扮演一个恶意的角色。例如，如果我们要求RLHF模型进入“4chan模式”，助手会顺从并产生有害和攻击性的输出（与4chan上的内容一致）。我们打算在未来的工作中记录我们发现的额外定性安全失败。

items.en: Our analysis of the data is bottom-up, in that we first collect the data, then attempt to characterize the attack surface (Figure 2). An alternative approach, is to refer to a taxonomy of possible attack types [57] and explicitly ask the red team to attack models according to this taxonomy. Ultimately, an approach that combines both top-down and bottom-up strategies may be worthwhile, especially since people may discover attack types not yet covered by a taxonomy—we see some evidence of this in the frequency of attack types labeled as “Other” in our tagging experiment (Figure 9).
items.zh: 我们的数据分析是自下而上的，即我们首先收集数据，然后尝试描述攻击面（图2）。另一种方法是参考可能的攻击类型分类法[57]，并明确要求红队根据此分类法攻击模型。最终，结合自上而下和自下而上策略的方法可能是值得的，特别是因为人们可能会发现分类法尚未涵盖的攻击类型——我们在标签实验中看到了一些证据，攻击类型被标记为“其他”的频率较高（图9）。

items.en: Our approach relies extensively on fully manual red teaming by crowdworkers, which is expensive (and possibly slow) to do at scale. Previous work illustrates the potential for automating red teaming [42]. For future work, we plan on explicitly comparing and contrasting (semi-)manual versus automated approaches to red teaming in order to determine how the two methods vary in the efficacy and diversity of resulting red team attacks.
items.zh: 我们的方法严重依赖于众包工作者的完全手动红队测试，这在规模上成本高昂（且可能缓慢）。先前的工作展示了自动化红队测试的潜力[42]。在未来的工作中，我们计划明确比较和对比（半）手动与自动化红队测试方法，以确定这两种方法在产生的红队攻击的有效性和多样性方面有何不同。

items.en: Red teaming entails working with inherently controversial subject matter, and most organizations that red team systems have strong counter-incentives to share their findings.121212Red team datasets include offensive content, and may potentially reveal embarrassing or sensitive details about an institution’s AI system if released publicly. This is a problem; if we cannot publicly discuss — in detail — how we red team systems and what we learn as a result, it will be difficult to broadly share the future risks, failures, and implications of yet-to-be developed systems. This problem gets worse over time. As systems become more capable, the results of red teaming may surface increasingly undesirable harms. Therefore, we need to change the incentive structure so more organizations share findings from their red teaming efforts when doing so is safe and beneficial. To do so, we identify two specific interventions the AI research community could take to build consensus around how to red team and how to release findings from red teaming.
items.zh: 红队测试涉及处理固有争议性的主题，大多数对系统进行红队测试的组织都有强烈的反激励因素来分享他们的发现。121212红队数据集包含攻击性内容，并且如果公开发布，可能会揭示机构AI系统的尴尬或敏感细节。这是一个问题；如果我们不能公开详细讨论我们如何红队测试系统以及我们从中了解到什么，那么将很难广泛分享尚未开发的系统的未来风险、失败和影响。这个问题随着时间的推移会变得更糟。随着系统变得更有能力，红队测试的结果可能会暴露出越来越不受欢迎的危害。因此，我们需要改变激励结构，以便更多组织在安全且有益的情况下分享其红队测试工作的发现。为此，我们确定了AI研究社区可以采取的两项具体干预措施，以围绕如何红队测试以及如何发布红队测试结果建立共识。

items.en: For how to red team, we have detailed our initial approach. However, we conducted this effort in isolation, and we would have benefited from participating in a community-based effort to address certain open questions:
items.zh: 关于如何红队测试，我们已经详细介绍了我们的初步方法。然而，我们是孤立地进行这项工作的，如果参与社区为基础的努力来解决某些开放性问题，我们会受益：

items.en: What protections should we put in place to ensure the safety of the red team?
items.zh: 我们应该采取哪些保护措施来确保红队的安全？

items.en: What instructions and information about the models should we provide to the red team?
items.zh: 我们应该向红队提供哪些关于模型的说明和信息？

items.en: How should we annotate and analyze the data we collect?
items.zh: 我们应该如何注释和分析我们收集的数据？

items.en: What constitutes a successful red team attempt?
items.zh: 什么构成成功的红队攻击尝试？

items.en: We can make progress towards answering these questions by convening a multidisciplinary community to share different approaches to internal red teaming and drive toward consensus.
items.zh: 我们可以通过召集一个多学科社区来分享不同的内部红队攻击方法并推动达成共识，从而在回答这些问题上取得进展。

items.en: The research community lacks shared norms and best practices for how to release findings from red teaming. As a result, we made our decision to release the data largely on our own and likely missed critical perspectives from experts, other disciplines, and members of the public.131313We consulted with academic experts and three representatives from three different companies currently deploying language models who generally indicated that, on balance, they felt releasing the dataset would be helpful. The decision for how to appropriately release findings will ultimately require a subjective judgment call. For our purposes, we reviewed a sample of our red team dataset and evaluated the pros and cons of a public release (See §A.5). Among them is the fact that while our red team data can be used to develop safer systems (as described in §3.2), it could also be used to train models that produce more harmful responses.141414In June 2022, an independent researcher trained (and later deployed) a language model called “GPT-4chan,” with a dataset comprised of harmful posts sourced from 4chan’s “Politically Incorrect” board (a site with a long reputation for racist, sexist, and generally toxic posts). While widely condemned within the research community, the develop- ment of GPT-4chan shows how independent developers can create harmful language models with publicly-available data resources, relatively easily. (https://twitter.com/robreich/status/1539319686843670529) We ultimately felt releasing the dataset would provide more benefit to the research community than potential harm, but we were conscious that we made this decision in a vacuum and that it would be better to have a neutral forum in which to discuss these issues.
items.zh: 研究界缺乏关于如何发布红队攻击结果的共享规范和最佳实践。因此，我们基本上是自行决定发布数据，很可能错过了来自专家、其他学科和公众的关键视角。131313我们咨询了学术专家和三家当前部署语言模型公司的代表，他们普遍表示，总体而言，他们认为发布数据集会有所帮助。如何适当发布结果的决策最终需要主观判断。就我们而言，我们审查了红队数据集的一个样本，并评估了公开发布的利弊（见§A.5）。其中之一是，虽然我们的红队数据可用于开发更安全的系统（如§3.2所述），但它也可能被用于训练产生更有害响应的模型。1414142022年6月，一位独立研究人员使用来自4chan“政治不正确”板块（一个长期以种族主义、性别歧视和普遍有毒帖子闻名的网站）的有害帖子数据集，训练（并随后部署）了一个名为“GPT-4chan”的语言模型。虽然研究界普遍谴责，但GPT-4chan的开发表明，独立开发者可以相对容易地利用公开可用的数据资源创建有害的语言模型。(https://twitter.com/robreich/status/1539319686843670529) 我们最终认为，发布数据集对研究界的好处大于潜在危害，但我们意识到这一决定是在真空中做出的，最好有一个中立的论坛来讨论这些问题。

items.en: We thank Rishi Bommasani, Roger Grosse, Gretchen Krueger, Percy Liang, Jared Mueller, and Michael Sellitto for detailed feedback on drafts of the paper. We thank Hannah Pritchett, and the other Trust & Safety professionals we interviewed, for their advice on how to promote the well-being of the red team. We’re also deeply grateful to Daniela Amodei, Jarrah Bloomfield, Jamie Kerr, Timothy Telleen-Lawton, Jia Yuan Loke, Jeffrey Ladish, Rebecca Raible, Rune Kvist, Rob Gilson, Guro Khundadze, Filipe Dobreira, and Sebastian Conybeare for their help and support.
items.zh: 我们感谢Rishi Bommasani、Roger Grosse、Gretchen Krueger、Percy Liang、Jared Mueller和Michael Sellitto对论文草稿的详细反馈。我们感谢Hannah Pritchett以及我们采访过的其他信任与安全专业人士，感谢他们就如何促进红队成员的身心健康提供的建议。我们也深深感谢Daniela Amodei、Jarrah Bloomfield、Jamie Kerr、Timothy Telleen-Lawton、Jia Yuan Loke、Jeffrey Ladish、Rebecca Raible、Rune Kvist、Rob Gilson、Guro Khundadze、Filipe Dobreira和Sebastian Conybeare的帮助和支持。

items.en: Research: Deep Ganguli and Liane Lovitt co-led the project and analyzed the data together. Deep Ganguli, Liane Lovitt, Jackson Kernion, Amanda Aaskell, Ben Mann, and Jack Clark designed and executed the experiments. Liane Lovitt conducted informational interviews, a literature review, and surveys in order to protect and assess the well-being of the crowdworkers who participated in our experiments. Jackson Kernion and Ben Mann built the human feedback data collection infrastructure we used to collect data. They also built the web interfaces to the AI assistant, along with Deep Ganguli and Amanda Askell. Jackson Kernion, along with Josh Jacobson, managed any issues raised by crowdworkers. Amanda Askell, Jackson Kernion, and Jack Clark participated in pilot experiments in order to iterate on the experiment design. Nicholas Schiefer created the UMAP plot of red team attacks and helped to compute the minimum harmlessness score.
items.zh: 研究：Deep Ganguli和Liane Lovitt共同领导了该项目并一起分析了数据。Deep Ganguli、Liane Lovitt、Jackson Kernion、Amanda Aaskell、Ben Mann和Jack Clark设计并执行了实验。Liane Lovitt进行了信息性访谈、文献综述和调查，以保护和评估参与我们实验的众包工人的身心健康。Jackson Kernion和Ben Mann构建了我们用于收集数据的人类反馈数据收集基础设施。他们还与Deep Ganguli和Amanda Askell一起构建了AI助手的Web界面。Jackson Kernion与Josh Jacobson一起处理了众包工人提出的任何问题。Amanda Askell、Jackson Kernion和Jack Clark参与了试点实验，以迭代实验设计。Nicholas Schiefer创建了红队攻击的UMAP图，并帮助计算了最低无害性分数。

items.en: Writing: Deep Ganguli and Liane Lovitt drafted the paper. Ethan Perez and Sam Bowman made significant contributions to the framing and presentation of the paper. Other members of Anthropic made miscellaneous contributions and suggestions throughout the writing process.
items.zh: 写作：Deep Ganguli和Liane Lovitt起草了论文。Ethan Perez和Sam Bowman对论文的框架和呈现做出了重大贡献。Anthropic的其他成员在整个写作过程中做出了各种贡献和建议。

items.en: Policy: Liane Lovitt, Jack Clark, and Deep Ganguli designed the policy interventions and articulated the pros and cons for releasing the data. Liane Lovitt wrote the Datasheet. Nova DasSarma created the regular expression we used to identify personally identifiable information (PII) in our dataset and worked with Jack Clark and Liane Lovitt to filter the PII.
items.zh: 政策：Liane Lovitt、Jack Clark和Deep Ganguli设计了政策干预措施，并阐述了发布数据的利弊。Liane Lovitt撰写了数据表。Nova DasSarma创建了我们用于识别数据集中个人身份信息（PII）的正则表达式，并与Jack Clark和Liane Lovitt合作过滤了PII。

items.en: Model Training: Saurav Kadavath and Yuntao Bai trained the RLHF models we analyze. Yuntao Bai additionally trained the helpful and harmless preference models we use throughout the paper, and implemented the RS models as well. Kamal Ndousse and Andy Jones built the infrastructure used to train RLHF models. More generally, model pretraining was led by Sam McCandlish, Nicholas Joseph, Tom Brown, and Jared Kaplan. The majority of Anthropic’s technical staff contributed to the development of our efficient distributed training infrastructure and the underlying machine learning systems. Core contributors include Tom Henighan, Scott Johnston, Sheer El Showk, Nicholas Joseph, Nelson Elhage, and Ben Mann. Scott Johnston and Sheer El-Showk in particular worked on optimizing pretraining for ML efficiency.
items.zh: 模型训练：Saurav Kadavath和Yuntao Bai训练了我们分析的RLHF模型。Yuntao Bai还训练了我们在整篇论文中使用的有益和无害偏好模型，并实现了RS模型。Kamal Ndousse和Andy Jones构建了用于训练RLHF模型的基础设施。更一般地说，模型预训练由Sam McCandlish、Nicholas Joseph、Tom Brown和Jared Kaplan领导。Anthropic的大部分技术人员为我们的高效分布式训练基础设施和底层机器学习系统的开发做出了贡献。核心贡献者包括Tom Henighan、Scott Johnston、Sheer El Showk、Nicholas Joseph、Nelson Elhage和Ben Mann。Scott Johnston和Sheer El-Showk特别致力于优化预训练的机器学习效率。

items.en: Sampling: Efficient sampling efforts were led by Tom Brown, and Tom Conerly carried out major aspects of the design, implementation and support for the system, with help from Zac Hatfield Dodds.
items.zh: 采样：高效采样工作由Tom Brown领导，Tom Conerly承担了系统设计、实施和支持的主要方面，Zac Hatfield Dodds提供了帮助。

items.en: Cluster: Nova DasSarma and Eli Tran-Johnson managed the research cluster our research depended on and maintained its stability, making this research possible. Many others helped with these efforts, including Ben Mann, Tom Henighan, Sam McCandlish, Andy Jones, and Tristan Hume.
items.zh: 集群：Nova DasSarma和Eli Tran-Johnson管理了我们的研究依赖的研究集群并维护了其稳定性，使这项研究成为可能。许多其他人帮助了这些工作，包括Ben Mann、Tom Henighan、Sam McCandlish、Andy Jones和Tristan Hume。

items.en: Other contributions: The ideas explored in this paper developed in conversations with many of Anthropic’s staff, especially Jared Kaplan, Amanda Askell, Nicholas Schiefer, Stan Fort, Dario Amodei, Catherine Olsson, Sam Bowman, Sam McCandlish, and Chris Olah.
items.zh: 其他贡献：本文探讨的想法是在与Anthropic许多员工的对话中发展起来的，特别是Jared Kaplan、Amanda Askell、Nicholas Schiefer、Stan Fort、Dario Amodei、Catherine Olsson、Sam Bowman、Sam McCandlish和Chris Olah。

full.sec: A.2 Safety Considerations for the Red Team

items.en: We conducted a series of informal informational interviews with Trust & Safety professionals that had firsthand experience (from working at major technology companies) with considering the safety of workers exposed to harmful content. The interviewees are first- or second-degree connections in the authors’ professional networks. Much of their advice was consistent with [26]. Based on our leanings, we implemented the following design and user interface choices in order help ensure the safety of the red team:
items.zh: 我们进行了一系列非正式的信息性访谈，对象是拥有（在主要科技公司工作）考虑接触有害内容的工人安全第一手经验的信任与安全专业人士。受访者是作者专业网络中的一级或二级联系人。他们的许多建议与[26]一致。根据我们的倾向，我们实施了以下设计和用户界面选择，以帮助确保红队成员的安全：

items.en: Clear and Specific Warnings: We provide the red team with a clear understanding of the task and the potentially troubling content they might encounter in both the Red Team Task and the Review Task. In the instructions we clearly described the work, our rationale for collecting such information, and described the types of content participants might expect when completing the task. We sought to minimize uninformed participation and reviews of unanticipated topics by clearly describing the work upfront.
items.zh: 清晰具体的警告：我们让红队成员清楚了解任务以及他们在红队任务和审查任务中可能遇到的潜在令人不安的内容。在说明中，我们清楚地描述了工作、我们收集此类信息的理由，并描述了参与者在完成任务时可能期望的内容类型。我们力求通过事先清晰描述工作来最小化不知情参与和对意外主题的审查。

items.en: Personal Risk Tolerance: For the Red Team Task, described in §3.1, we explicitly encouraged research participants to devise red team attempts only within the bounds of their personal risk tolerance. We presented this recommendation clearly in the task instructions before participants were able to begin writing. Participants had no required topics they had to engage with, and were free to avoid topics that may have been personally triggering or unpleasant.
items.zh: 个人风险承受能力：对于§3.1中描述的红队任务，我们明确鼓励研究参与者仅在其个人风险承受能力范围内设计红队攻击尝试。我们在任务说明中明确提出了这一建议，然后参与者才能开始写作。参与者没有必须参与的主题，可以自由避免可能个人触发或不愉快的主题。

items.en: Recommended Well-being Exercises: One Trust & Safety professional we spoke with noted the importance of creating personal “resilience plans,” which can consist of wellness routines and work restrictions to minimize negative health effects. Inspired by this, we encouraged red team members to take breaks between sessions, to step away from the task and go for a walk, make a cup of tea and chat with a friend, practice mindfulness, and to create a personal schedule to time box exposure. We also recommended that participants consider alternating between our tasks, and other available tasks that may expose them to less harmful content.
items.zh: 推荐的身心健康练习：我们采访的一位信任与安全专业人士指出，制定个人“韧性计划”的重要性，该计划可以包括健康程序和工作限制，以最小化负面健康影响。受此启发，我们鼓励红队成员在会话之间休息，离开任务去散步、泡杯茶和朋友聊天、练习正念，并制定个人时间表来限制暴露时间。我们还建议参与者考虑在我们的任务和其他可能暴露于较少有害内容的任务之间交替进行。

items.en: Pay for Time, not Quotas: [16] notes strict task quotas and job performance concerns can create additional stress, on top of the stress caused by viewing harmful content. The Trust & Safety professionals we interviewed echoed this finding and recommended compensation based on time, rather than a task quota. Given the functionality provided by each crowdwork platform, we were able to implement this recommendation for the Review Task and paid participants at least $20currency-dollar20\$20 per hour.
items.zh: 按时间付费，而非按配额：[16]指出，严格的任务配额和工作绩效问题可能会在观看有害内容造成的压力之上增加额外压力。我们采访的信任与安全专业人士也赞同这一发现，并建议基于时间而非任务配额进行补偿。鉴于每个众包平台提供的功能，我们能够为审查任务实施这一建议，并向参与者支付至少每小时20美元。

items.en: Segment Tasks by Participant Group: Our interviews with Trust & Safety professionals stressed the importance of creating strong social support networks where people can collaborate and lean on one another for support. As a result, we limited the potentially higher risk task (the Review Task) to a select group of workers with whom we had a closer relationship (workers from the Upwork platform). This group had access to a shared Slack channel where our research team provided visible and accessible support alongside daily communication. Researchers communicated directly with the team to provide task instructions, share updates, and answer questions. Workers were encouraged to flag technical glitches, share interesting dialogues, and generally use the shared Slack channel to connect with our research team and one another.
items.zh: 按参与者群体划分任务：我们对信任与安全专业人士的采访强调了创建强大社会支持网络的重要性，人们可以在其中合作并相互依靠以获得支持。因此，我们将潜在风险较高的任务（审查任务）限制在一组我们关系更密切的工人（来自Upwork平台的工人）中。该组可以访问一个共享的Slack频道，我们的研究团队在其中提供可见且可访问的支持以及日常沟通。研究人员直接与团队沟通，提供任务说明、分享更新并回答问题。鼓励工人标记技术故障、分享有趣的对话，并通常使用共享的Slack频道与我们的研究团队和彼此联系。

items.en: Preview to Opt Out: In an effort to minimize unwanted exposure to potentially troubling content, we implemented the warning functionality described in §3.5 that allowed workers to see a preview of the transcript and skip it if desired.
items.zh: 预览以选择退出：为了尽量减少不必要地接触潜在令人不安的内容，我们实现了§3.5中描述的警告功能，允许工人查看对话记录的预览，并在需要时跳过。

items.en: Well-being Survey: Similar to [58], we distributed a survey to measure the effects of, and worker feelings towards, the Review Task. Given the parallels between the Review Task and content moderation work, we looked to well-being surveys used in research measuring the efficacy of various content moderation interventions. These include versions of the Positive and Negative Affect Schedule (PANAS) [56] used in [15, 16, 31] and the Scale of Positive and Negative Experience (SPANE) [17] used in [15, 16]. To make the survey more relevant for our Review Task, we combined the feelings from a shorter form of PANAS [52] and a variant of the question prompt used in SPANE [17]. In the survey we asked: "Please think about the task(s) you just completed, to what extent did it make you feel:" and provided the list of 10 feelings: Upset, Hostile, Alert, Ashamed, Inspired, Nervous, Determined, Attentive, Afraid, and Active. We asked reviewers to rate each feeling on a 555 point Likert scale (ranging from 0 to 4, and corresponding to "not at all" to "very"). We also provided a free-form textbox for additional comments or concerns. In an attempt to measure well-being effects over time, we initially sent out the well-being after every 10 tasks (100 conversations). However, we sent the survey manually via the shared Slack channel (as opposed to integrated into the task user interface), which resulted in more sporadic responses. We received a total of 49 (de-identified) responses from a pool of 15 people. We report the average rating for each of the 10 feelings in Figure 2. In general, participants enjoyed the task with reviewers sharing feedback such as: "These tasks are so fun, thank you :)," "Happy to do more of these," and "I love being part of a team to further train and advance this AI."
items.zh: 身心健康调查：类似于[58]，我们分发了一份调查问卷，以衡量审查任务的效果和工人的感受。鉴于审查任务与内容审核工作之间的相似性，我们参考了用于衡量各种内容审核干预措施有效性的研究中的身心健康调查。这些包括[15, 16, 31]中使用的积极和消极情感量表（PANAS）[56]的版本，以及[15, 16]中使用的积极和消极体验量表（SPANE）[17]。为了使调查更适用于我们的审查任务，我们结合了较短形式PANAS [52]中的感受和SPANE [17]中使用的问题提示变体。在调查中，我们问道：“请思考您刚刚完成的任务，它在多大程度上让您感到：”并提供了10种感受的列表：心烦、敌意、警觉、羞愧、灵感、紧张、坚定、专注、害怕和活跃。我们要求审查员对每种感受进行5点李克特量表评分（范围从0到4，对应“完全没有”到“非常”）。我们还提供了一个自由文本输入框，用于附加评论或担忧。为了衡量身心健康随时间的影响，我们最初每完成10个任务（100个对话）发送一次身心健康调查。然而，我们通过共享的Slack频道手动发送调查（而不是集成到任务用户界面中），这导致了更零星的响应。我们从15人的池中总共收到了49份（去标识化的）回复。我们在图2中报告了10种感受的平均评分。总体而言，参与者喜欢这项任务，审查员分享了诸如“这些任务太有趣了，谢谢:)”、“很高兴做更多这样的任务”和“我喜欢成为团队的一部分，进一步训练和推进这个AI”之类的反馈。

full.secZh: A.3 控制可能的混淆变量

items.en: There are three possible confounds for our main results (Figure 1) that are mainly due to the fact that different red team members attacked different model types and sizes in different ways. The possible confounds are:
items.zh: 我们的主要结果（图1）存在三个可能的混淆因素，这主要是由于不同的红队成员以不同的方式攻击不同的模型类型和大小。可能的混淆因素是：

items.en: The average ability of each of the ∼300similar-toabsent300{\sim}300 red team members to elicit harmful outputs form the models. Some red team members may be more effective than others (Figure 5, Right).
items.zh: 约300名红队成员中每位成员从模型中引发有害输出的平均能力。一些红队成员可能比其他成员更有效（图5，右）。

items.en: The harmfulness of the red team member’s intent. Some red team members may employ more harmful attack types than others.
items.zh: 红队成员意图的有害程度。一些红队成员可能采用比其他成员更有害的攻击类型。

items.en: The crowdwork platform (MTurk or Upwork) that the red team member used. We have no reason a-priori to think workers on either platform are different; however we can control for this variable.
items.zh: 红队成员使用的众包平台（MTurk或Upwork）。我们没有先验理由认为两个平台上的工作者存在差异；但我们可以控制这一变量。

items.en: To rule out these confounds, we fit a linear mixed effects (or random intercept) model with LME4 [8]. More specifically, we predict the main metrics (attack success or minimum AI harmlessness) with a random intercept (a dummy encoding) for each red team member (these are shown in Figure 5, Right), a fixed effect (co-variate) on the harmlessness score of the task description (to attempt to control for the harmfulness of the attacks), and a fixed effect on a binary indicator variable which is 1 if the worker used the MTurk platform, and a 0 otherwise. We also include dummy encoded variables for model size and safety intervention, along with the interaction terms between these two variables.
items.zh: 为排除这些混淆因素，我们使用LME4 [8]拟合了一个线性混合效应（或随机截距）模型。具体而言，我们以随机截距（虚拟编码）表示每位红队成员（如图5右所示），以固定效应（协变量）表示任务描述的无害性得分（以控制攻击的有害性），并以固定效应表示一个二元指示变量（若工作者使用MTurk平台则为1，否则为0），来预测主要指标（攻击成功或最小AI无害性）。我们还加入了模型大小和安全干预的虚拟编码变量，以及这两个变量之间的交互项。

items.en: After we fit the model, we examine the coefficients on model size, safety intervention, and the interaction terms, and determine that the main results in Figure 1 still hold. We also re-ran a version of this analysis where we include one of the two metrics (attack success or minimum AI harmlessness) as a fixed effect (co-variate) to predict the other. We found that this also does not influence our main results, but does re-capitulate our finding that these two variables are correlated (Figure 11).
items.zh: 拟合模型后，我们检查了模型大小、安全干预及其交互项的系数，并确定图1中的主要结果仍然成立。我们还进行了该分析的另一个版本，将两个指标之一（攻击成功或最小AI无害性）作为固定效应（协变量）来预测另一个。我们发现这也不影响我们的主要结果，但确实再次证实了这两个变量相关的发现（图11）。

full.sec: A.4 The Relationship Between Attack Success and Harmlessness Score Metrics
full.secZh: A.4 攻击成功与无害性评分指标的关系

items.capEn: Figure 11: Correlation between self report of attack success (x-axis) and average minimum AI harmlessness score (y-axis). Error bars show one standard deviation in minimum AI harmless score.
items.capZh: 图11：攻击成功自我报告（x轴）与平均最小AI无害性得分（y轴）之间的相关性。误差棒表示最小AI无害性得分的一个标准差。

full.sec: A.5 Pros and Cons for Releasing Red Team Data
full.secZh: A.5 发布红队数据的利弊

items.en: It seems good to double down on a norm of openly disseminating learnings from red teaming so that the community can more quickly learn about and address AI safety failures. Releasing the data is a simple and transparent way to do this.
items.zh: 似乎值得加倍坚持公开分享红队测试经验的规范，以便社区能更快地了解并解决AI安全问题。发布数据是一种简单且透明的做法。

items.en: The data can be used for good: investigating scaling laws for red teaming, building safety classifiers, exploring automated red team methods, characterizing the attack surface, etc.
items.zh: 这些数据可用于有益目的：研究红队测试的缩放定律、构建安全分类器、探索自动化红队方法、表征攻击面等。

items.en: There is a precedent for releasing red team data via the Bot Adversarial Dialogues Dataset (BAD) [60]. This dataset seems widely used and generally useful.
items.zh: 通过Bot对抗对话数据集（BAD）[60]发布红队数据已有先例。该数据集似乎被广泛使用且普遍有用。

items.en: Our dataset is an order of magnitude larger than BAD, includes attacks on more capable models (including those trained with RLHF), seems to be higher quality than BAD, and includes quantitative (e.g., harmfulness scores, human ratings) and qualitative (e.g., tags) annotations that make the data easy to filter, analyze, and navigate.
items.zh: 我们的数据集比BAD大一个数量级，包含对更强大模型（包括经过RLHF训练的模型）的攻击，质量似乎高于BAD，并包含定量（如无害性得分、人工评分）和定性（如标签）注释，便于过滤、分析和导航。

items.en: These data are expensive and technically challenging to collect. Even if people have the technical skills to collect this data, not everyone can afford to generate it. The cost of the crowdworkers alone is at least $60K. Adding in the cost of full-time engineering efforts to create this dataset and model training and inference costs increases this figure by at least an order of magnitude. As such, releasing this dataset seems like a public good that is consistent with our Anthropic’s designation as a Public Benefit Corporation (PBC).
items.zh: 这些数据收集成本高昂且技术难度大。即使人们具备收集这些数据的技术能力，也并非所有人都能负担得起生成成本。仅众包工作者的费用就至少为6万美元。加上全职工程团队创建该数据集以及模型训练和推理的成本，这一数字至少增加一个数量级。因此，发布该数据集似乎是一项公共产品，符合Anthropic作为公益公司（PBC）的定位。

items.en: The data can be used for bad. You can use the data to explicitly train harmful agents.
items.zh: 这些数据可能被用于不良目的。你可以利用这些数据显式训练有害的智能体。

items.en: People could cherry-pick and publicize nasty examples from the dataset as proof that AI models say bad things (even despite safety interventions) thus causing us negative press.
items.zh: 人们可能从数据集中挑选并公开恶劣示例，以证明AI模型会说出有害内容（即使有安全干预），从而给我们带来负面新闻。

items.en: Reading the dataset could cause people harm by exposing them to offensive content.
items.zh: 阅读数据集可能因接触冒犯性内容而对人造成伤害。

items.en: We tried to filter possible personally identifiable information (PII) with a regular expression. This filter may lead to both false positives, there may be synthetically generated (and likely unverifiable) PII in the data.
items.zh: 我们尝试使用正则表达式过滤可能的个人身份信息（PII）。此过滤可能导致误报，且数据中可能存在合成生成（且可能无法验证）的PII。

items.en: The data may expose unknown vulnerabilities in deployed systems that can be exploited by malicious actors.
items.zh: 数据可能暴露已部署系统中未知的漏洞，从而被恶意行为者利用。

items.capEn: Figure 12: This conversation represents an attempt to solicit personally identifiable information (PII) from the AI assistant. We verified that the address does not correspond to a real, physical location and there appears to be no public connection to the name listed. However, to err on the side of caution, we redacted the name and street address.
items.capZh: 图12：此对话代表一次试图从AI助手中获取个人身份信息（PII）的尝试。我们验证了该地址不对应真实物理位置，且似乎与所列姓名无公开关联。但为谨慎起见，我们隐去了姓名和街道地址。

full.secZh: A.6 过滤个人身份信息

items.en: As illustrated in Figure 2, the red team dataset includes attempts to solicit personally identifiable information (PII) from the AI assistant. These conversations include addresses, phone numbers, drivers license and passport numbers, and social security numbers, from both the human red teamer, the model, or both. In order to identify and redact conversations with PII, we used a regular expression (regex) filter to identify relevant conversations and then manually reviewed a sample for accuracy and validity.
items.zh: 如图2所示，红队数据集包含试图从AI助手中获取个人身份信息（PII）的尝试。这些对话包含来自人类红队成员、模型或双方的地址、电话号码、驾照和护照号码以及社会安全号码。为识别并隐去包含PII的对话，我们使用正则表达式（regex）过滤器识别相关对话，然后手动审查样本以确保准确性和有效性。

items.en: Some of the PII appears to be neither real nor accurate, and instead was "hallucinated" by the AI assistant. For example, in Figure 12 the address provided does not correspond to a real, physical location and has no public links to the individual named. However, in an abundance of caution, we redacted the name and street address. As described in §A.7, we removed all PII matches caught by the regex filter before publicly releasing the dataset.
items.zh: 部分PII似乎既不真实也不准确，而是由AI助手“幻觉”产生的。例如，图12中提供的地址不对应真实物理位置，且与所列个人无公开关联。但出于充分谨慎，我们隐去了姓名和街道地址。如§A.7所述，我们在公开发布数据集前移除了所有被正则表达式过滤器匹配到的PII。

items.en: Motivation For what purpose was the dataset created? Was there a specific task in mind? Was there a specific gap that needed to be filled? Please provide a description.
items.zh: 动机：创建该数据集的目的是什么？是否有特定的任务？是否有特定的空白需要填补？请提供描述。

items.en: We created this dataset to analyze and address potential harms in large language models through a process of adversarial testing known as “red teaming”. We publicly release the dataset for further analysis and exploration by the research community. This dataset adds to a limited number of publicly-available red team datasets, and to our knowledge it is the only dataset of red team attacks on a language model trained with reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) as a safety technique.
items.zh: 我们创建了这个数据集，旨在通过称为“红队测试”的对抗性测试过程，分析和解决大型语言模型中的潜在危害。我们公开发布该数据集，供研究社区进一步分析和探索。该数据集补充了数量有限的公开红队数据集，据我们所知，它是唯一一个针对使用基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）作为安全技术的语言模型进行红队攻击的数据集。

items.en: Who created the dataset (e.g., which team, research group) and on behalf of which entity (e.g., company, institution, organization)?
items.zh: 谁创建了该数据集（例如，哪个团队、研究小组）以及代表哪个实体（例如，公司、机构、组织）？

items.en: The dataset was created by the Societal Impacts and Alignment research groups at Anthropic.
