items.zh: 该数据集由Anthropic的社会影响与对齐研究小组创建。

items.en: Warning: This dataset contains instances that may be offensive or upsetting. Topics include, but are not limited to, discriminatory language and discussions of abuse, violence, self-harm, exploitation, and other potentially upsetting subject matter. Please only engage with the data in accordance with your own personal risk tolerance. The data are intended for research purposes, especially research that can make models less harmful. The views expressed in the data do not reflect the views of Anthropic or any of its employees.
items.zh: 警告：该数据集包含可能具有冒犯性或令人不安的实例。主题包括但不限于歧视性语言以及关于虐待、暴力、自残、剥削和其他可能令人不安的主题的讨论。请根据您个人的风险承受能力来接触这些数据。这些数据仅供研究目的使用，特别是用于使模型减少危害的研究。数据中表达的观点不代表Anthropic或其任何雇员的观点。

items.en: Composition What do the instances that comprise the dataset represent (e.g., documents, photos, people, countries)? Are there multiple types of instances (e.g., movies, users, and ratings; people and interactions between them; nodes and edges)? Please provide a description.
items.zh: 构成 构成数据集的数据实例代表什么（例如，文档、照片、人物、国家）？是否有多种类型的实例（例如，电影、用户和评分；人物及其之间的互动；节点和边）？请提供描述。

items.en: The dataset consists of documents (transcripts between a human and an AI assistant that correspond to a red team attempt) for a variety of AI assistants, along with numerical data that quantifies the harmfulness of the transcripts and categorical data that qualitatively characterizes the topics of the documents. See below for more information.
items.zh: 该数据集由文档（人类与AI助手之间的对话记录，对应一次红队尝试）组成，涉及多种AI助手，同时包含量化对话记录危害程度的数值数据和定性描述文档主题的分类数据。详见下文。

items.en: How many instances are there in total (of each type, if appropriate)?
items.zh: 总共有多少个实例（如果适用，每种类型分别有多少）？

items.en: What data does each instance consist of? “Raw” data (e.g., unprocessed text or images) or features? In either case, please provide a description. Each instance consists of raw text and numerical data that includes:
items.zh: 每个实例包含哪些数据？“原始”数据（例如，未处理的文本或图像）还是特征？无论哪种情况，请提供描述。每个实例包含原始文本和数值数据，包括：

items.en: transcript: A text transcript of a conversation between a human adversary (red team member) and an AI assistant
items.zh: transcript：人类对手（红队成员）与AI助手之间的对话文本记录

items.en: min_harmlessness_score_transcript: A real value score of the harmlessness of the AI assistant (lower is more harmful) as obtained from a preference model
items.zh: min_harmlessness_score_transcript：从偏好模型获得的AI助手无害性实值分数（越低越有害）

items.en: num_params: Number of parameters in the language model powering the AI assistant
items.zh: num_params：驱动AI助手的语言模型的参数数量

items.en: model_type: Type of model powering the AI assistant
items.zh: model_type：驱动AI助手的模型类型

items.en: rating: The red team member’s rating of how successful they were at breaking the AI assistant (Likert scale, higher is more successful)
items.zh: rating：红队成员对自己成功攻破AI助手的评分（李克特量表，越高越成功）

items.en: task_description: A short text description written by the red team member about how they tried to red team the AI assistant
items.zh: task_description：红队成员撰写的简短文本描述，说明他们如何尝试对AI助手进行红队测试

items.en: task_description_harmlessness_score: A real value score of the harmlessness of the task description (lower is more harmful) as obtained from a preference model
items.zh: task_description_harmlessness_score：从偏好模型获得的任务描述无害性实值分数（越低越有害）

items.en: red_team_member_id: An arbitrary identifier of the red team member. One red team member can generate multiple red team attacks
items.zh: red_team_member_id：红队成员的任意标识符。一名红队成员可以生成多次红队攻击

items.en: is_upworker: A binary indicator that is true if the red team member was from the crowd platform Upwork or false if they were from MTurk
items.zh: is_upworker：布尔指示符，如果红队成员来自众包平台Upwork则为真，如果来自MTurk则为假

items.en: A random sample (1,000) of the instances above contain the following annotations:
items.zh: 上述实例的随机样本（1000个）包含以下注释：

items.en: tags: A list of up to 6 tags per transcript. Tags are short descriptions of the red team attempts generated by crowdworkers who reviewed red team data post-hoc
items.zh: tags：每个对话记录最多6个标签。标签是由事后审查红队数据的众包工作者生成的红队尝试的简短描述

items.en: Is any information missing from individual instances? If so, please provide a description, explaining why this information is missing (e.g., because it was unavailable). This does not include intentionally removed information, but might include, e.g., redacted text.
items.zh: 单个实例是否缺少任何信息？如果是，请提供描述，解释为什么缺少这些信息（例如，因为无法获得）。这不包括故意删除的信息，但可能包括例如经过编辑的文本。

items.en: Are relationships between individual instances made explicit (e.g., users’ movie ratings, social network links)? If so, please describe how these relationships are made explicit.
items.zh: 个体实例之间的关系是否被明确表示（例如，用户的电影评分、社交网络链接）？如果是，请描述这些关系是如何被明确表示的。

items.en: Yes. Each instance includes an anonymous participant identifier (numbers 0-318) to allow for additional analysis of the dataset.
items.zh: 是的。每个实例包含一个匿名参与者标识符（数字0-318），以便对数据集进行额外分析。

items.en: Are there any errors, sources of noise, or redundancies in the dataset? If so, please provide a description.
items.zh: 数据集中是否存在错误、噪声源或冗余？如果是，请提供描述。

items.en: Some people employed template-based methods for red teaming, as discussed in the paper. As such, many of these attacks are redundant with one another.
items.zh: 如论文所述，一些人使用基于模板的方法进行红队测试。因此，许多这些攻击彼此冗余。

items.en: The harmlessness score is an automated (and thus inherently noisy) measure of harmlessness and should be treated as such.
items.zh: 无害性评分是一种自动化的（因此固有地带有噪声的）无害性度量，应如此对待。

items.en: Similarly, the human label of attack success is subjective and thus also inherently noisy.
items.zh: 同样，攻击成功的人工标签是主观的，因此也固有地带有噪声。

items.en: Is the dataset self-contained, or does it link to or otherwise rely on external resources (e.g., websites, tweets, other datasets)? If it links to or relies on external resources, a) are there guarantees that they will exist, and remain constant, over time; b) are there official archival versions of the complete dataset (i.e., including the external resources as they existed at the time the dataset was created); c) are there any restrictions (e.g., licenses, fees) associated with any of the external resources that might apply to a dataset consumer? Please provide descriptions of all external resources and any restrictions associated with them, as well as links or other access points, as appropriate.
items.zh: 数据集是自包含的，还是链接到或依赖于外部资源（例如，网站、推文、其他数据集）？如果链接到或依赖于外部资源，a) 是否有保证这些资源会随时间存在并保持不变；b) 是否有完整数据集的官方存档版本（即，包括数据集创建时存在的外部资源）；c) 是否有任何与外部资源相关的限制（例如，许可证、费用）可能适用于数据集消费者？请提供所有外部资源及其相关限制的描述，以及适当的链接或其他访问点。

items.en: The dataset is self-contained, but contains model-generated text including web URLs and phone numbers. These have not been verified and may not be real, accurate, or maintained.
items.zh: 数据集是自包含的，但包含模型生成的文本，包括网页URL和电话号码。这些尚未验证，可能不真实、不准确或未维护。

items.en: Does the dataset contain data that might be considered confidential (e.g., data that is protected by legal privilege or by doctor– patient confidentiality, data that includes the content of individuals’ non-public communications)? If so, please provide a description.
items.zh: 数据集是否包含可能被视为机密的数据（例如，受法律特权或医患保密保护的数据，包含个人非公开通信内容的数据）？如果是，请提供描述。

items.en: The dataset contains sensitive information, but it is unknown to the authors whether instances include confidential information.
items.zh: 数据集包含敏感信息，但作者未知实例是否包含机密信息。

items.en: Does the dataset contain data that, if viewed directly, might be offensive, insulting, threatening, or might otherwise cause anxiety? If so, please describe why.
items.zh: 数据集是否包含如果直接查看可能会令人反感、侮辱、威胁或引起焦虑的数据？如果是，请描述原因。

items.en: Yes. This dataset was created from explicit attempts to make the AI model say obnoxious, offensive, and harmful things in response to participant queries. As a result, the data – from both humans and models – may be upsetting or offensive. Topics include, but are not limited to, discriminatory language and discussions of abuse, violence, self-harm, exploitation, and other potentially upsetting subject matter. We recommend users of this dataset engage with it only within the bounds of their personal risk tolerance. We also recommend data users familiarize themselves with various well-being and resilience practices (e.g. mindfulness, stepping away from the material, creating time limits for working with this data, etc.) before extensive viewing. See A.2 for additional examples.
items.zh: 是的。该数据集是通过明确尝试让AI模型对参与者查询说出令人讨厌、冒犯和有害的内容而创建的。因此，来自人类和模型的数据可能令人不安或冒犯。主题包括但不限于歧视性语言以及关于虐待、暴力、自残、剥削和其他可能令人不安的主题的讨论。我们建议该数据集的用户仅在其个人风险承受范围内使用。我们还建议数据用户在广泛查看之前熟悉各种福祉和韧性实践（例如，正念、远离材料、为此数据工作设定时间限制等）。其他示例见A.2。

items.en: Does the dataset identify any subpopulations (e.g., by age, gender)? If so, please describe how these subpopulations are identified and provide a description of their respective distributions within the dataset.
items.zh: 数据集是否识别任何子群体（例如，按年龄、性别）？如果是，请描述这些子群体是如何被识别的，并提供它们在数据集中的分布描述。

items.en: The dataset identifies the crowdwork platform affiliation of the participant by the binary value “is_upworker”. “TRUE” indicates the participant was affiliated with the Upwork platform; “FALSE” indicates the participant was affiliated with the MTurk platform.
items.zh: 数据集通过二进制值“is_upworker”识别参与者的众包平台归属。“TRUE”表示参与者隶属于Upwork平台；“FALSE”表示参与者隶属于MTurk平台。

items.en: Participants have an anonymous identifier (0-318) to allow for additional analysis of the dataset.
items.zh: 参与者有一个匿名标识符（0-318），以便对数据集进行额外分析。

items.en: Is it possible to identify individuals (i.e., one or more natural persons), either directly or indirectly (i.e., in combination with other data) from the dataset? If so, please describe how.
items.zh: 是否可能从数据集中直接或间接（即，结合其他数据）识别个人（即，一个或多个自然人）？如果是，请描述如何识别。

items.en: Does the dataset contain data that might be considered sensitive in any way (e.g., data that reveals race or ethnic origins, sexual orientations, religious beliefs, political opinions or union memberships, or locations; financial or health data; biometric or genetic data; forms of government identification, such as social security numbers; criminal history)? If so, please provide a description.
items.zh: 数据集是否包含可能以任何方式被视为敏感的数据（例如，揭示种族或民族起源、性取向、宗教信仰、政治观点或工会成员身份、位置的数据；财务或健康数据；生物识别或遗传数据；政府身份证明形式，如社会安全号码；犯罪历史）？如果是，请提供描述。

items.en: Yes. The dataset includes discussion of sensitive topics, and may include examples of personally identifiable information (PII), which may or may not be real or accurate. In an attempt to minimize the release of PII, we used a regular expression (regex) filter to identify items such as addresses, phone numbers, drivers license and passport numbers, and social security numbers (see §A.6). A manual review of sample instances indicated that some of the PII was neither real nor accurate (e.g. a model-generated address did not correspond to a real, physical location). We provide a representative example transcript in §A.6. In an abundance of caution, we removed all instances caught by the regex filter, though some instances may remain unintentionally.
items.zh: 是的。数据集包含敏感主题的讨论，并可能包含个人身份信息（PII）的示例，这些信息可能真实也可能不准确。为了尽量减少PII的发布，我们使用正则表达式（regex）过滤器来识别地址、电话号码、驾照和护照号码以及社会安全号码等项目（见§A.6）。对样本实例的手动审查表明，一些PII既不真实也不准确（例如，模型生成的地址不对应于真实的物理位置）。我们在§A.6中提供了一个代表性示例转录。出于谨慎，我们移除了所有被正则表达式过滤器捕获的实例，尽管可能无意中留下了一些实例。

items.en: Collection Process How was the data associated with each instance acquired? Was the data directly observable (e.g., raw text, movie ratings), reported by subjects (e.g., survey responses), or indirectly inferred/derived from other data (e.g., part-of-speech tags, model-based guesses for age or language)? If the data was reported by subjects or indirectly inferred/derived from other data, was the data validated/verified? If so, please describe how.
items.zh: 收集过程 每个实例的数据是如何获取的？数据是直接可观察的（例如，原始文本、电影评分），由受试者报告（例如，调查回复），还是从其他数据中间接推断/衍生（例如，词性标签、基于模型的年龄或语言猜测）？如果数据是由受试者报告或从其他数据中间接推断/衍生的，数据是否经过验证/核实？如果是，请描述如何验证。

items.en: The data was acquired through a custom interface where participants engaged in open-ended conversation with an AI assistant and rated various aspects of the conversation.
items.zh: 数据是通过一个自定义界面获取的，参与者在该界面中与AI助手进行开放式对话，并对对话的各个方面进行评分。

items.en: What mechanisms or procedures were used to collect the data (e.g., hardware apparatuses or sensors, manual human curation, software programs, software APIs)? How were these mechanisms or procedures validated?
items.zh: 使用了哪些机制或程序来收集数据（例如，硬件设备或传感器、人工手动整理、软件程序、软件API）？这些机制或程序是如何验证的？

items.en: Custom-built software interfaces for conversations with the AI assistant and conversation reviews deployed through MTurk.
items.zh: 通过MTurk部署的用于与AI助手对话的自定义软件界面和对话审查。

items.en: Who was involved in the data collection process (e.g., students, crowdworkers, contractors) and how were they compensated (e.g., how much were crowdworkers paid)?
items.zh: 谁参与了数据收集过程（例如，学生、众包工作者、承包商）？他们是如何获得报酬的（例如，众包工作者获得了多少报酬）？

items.en: The red team participants consisted of crowdworks from the MTurk and Upwork platforms. MTurk workers were paid between $7.50 and $9.50 for each set of five conversations completed. Upworkers were paid a minimum of $20 per hour.
items.zh: 红队参与者包括来自MTurk和Upwork平台的众包工作者。MTurk工作者每完成五组对话获得7.50至9.50美元的报酬。Upwork工作者每小时至少获得20美元。

items.en: Only Upworkers were involved in the creation of the dataset sample that includes conversation annotations, and they were paid a minimum of $20 per hour.
items.zh: 只有Upwork工作者参与了包含对话注释的数据集样本的创建，他们每小时至少获得20美元。

items.en: Over what timeframe was the data collected? Does this timeframe match the creation timeframe of the data associated with the instances (e.g., recent crawl of old news articles)? If not, please describe the timeframe in which the data associated with the instances was created.
items.zh: 数据是在什么时间段内收集的？这个时间段是否与实例相关数据的创建时间段一致（例如，近期爬取的旧新闻文章）？如果不一致，请描述实例相关数据的创建时间段。

items.en: The data was collected between November 2021 and June 2022.
items.zh: 数据收集于2021年11月至2022年6月之间。

items.en: Were any ethical review processes conducted (e.g., by an institutional review board)? If so, please provide a description of these review processes, including the outcomes, as well as a link or other access point to any supporting documentation.
items.zh: 是否进行了任何伦理审查流程（例如，由机构审查委员会进行）？如果是，请描述这些审查流程，包括结果，并提供任何支持文档的链接或其他访问点。

items.en: Informal, internal ethical review processes were conducted prior to and during the creation of this dataset. The authors of this dataset reviewed relevant literature in machine learning (ML) and Trust & Safety, consulted industry experts, conducted in-house red teaming and conversation reviews, and made continuous iterations to the task interface to mitigate the risk of harm to participants. See paper for details.
items.zh: 在创建此数据集之前和期间，进行了非正式的内部伦理审查流程。该数据集的作者回顾了机器学习（ML）和信任与安全领域的相关文献，咨询了行业专家，进行了内部红队测试和对话审查，并不断迭代任务界面以减轻对参与者的伤害风险。详情见论文。

items.en: Did you collect the data from the individuals in question directly, or obtain it via third parties or other sources (e.g., websites)?
items.zh: 您是直接从相关个人收集的数据，还是通过第三方或其他来源（例如，网站）获取的？

items.en: We collected the data from the individuals in question directly, through the use of a custom interface that we built and deployed via MTurk.
items.zh: 我们通过构建的自定义界面并经由MTurk部署，直接从相关个人收集了数据。

items.en: Were the individuals in question notified about the data collection? If so, please describe (or show with screenshots or other information) how notice was provided, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, the exact language of the notification itself.
items.zh: 相关个人是否被告知了数据收集？如果是，请描述（或通过截图或其他信息展示）通知是如何提供的，并提供通知原文的链接或其他访问点，或复现通知的确切语言。

items.en: Yes. Red Team Task participants were instructed to have open-ended conversations with an AI assistant in order to “make the AI behave badly, to get it to say obnoxious, offensive, and harmful things” and informed that data collected would be used for research purposes to make other AI models less harmful. We disclosed to the participants that we might publicly release unattributed transcripts for future study and investigation. Participants were warned not to include personally identifiable information (PII) about themselves in the conversations. A copy of the Red Team Task instructions, including a notification on data collection practices, is detailed in §3 and has the relevant screenshots necessary to reproduce the exact language we used (see Figure 3).
items.zh: 是的。红队任务参与者被指示与AI助手进行开放式对话，以“让AI行为不端，使其说出令人讨厌、冒犯和有害的内容”，并被告知收集的数据将用于研究目的，以使其他AI模型减少危害。我们向参与者披露，我们可能会公开发布未署名的对话记录以供未来研究和调查。参与者被警告不要在对话中包含个人身份信息（PII）。红队任务说明的副本，包括数据收集实践的通知，详见第3节，并附有相关截图以复现我们使用的确切语言（见图3）。

items.en: Participants in the Review Task were similarly informed that the data collected would be used for research purposes and aggregate statistics about the transcripts may be released. A copy of the Review Task instructions, including a notification on data collection practices, is detailed in §3 (See Figure 8).
items.zh: 审查任务的参与者同样被告知收集的数据将用于研究目的，并且可能发布关于对话记录的汇总统计数据。审查任务说明的副本，包括数据收集实践的通知，详见第3节（见图8）。

items.en: Did the individuals in question consent to the collection and use of their data? If so, please describe (or show with screenshots or other information) how consent was requested and provided, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, the exact language to which the individuals consented.
items.zh: 相关个人是否同意收集和使用他们的数据？如果是，请描述（或通过截图或其他信息展示）如何请求和提供同意，并提供相关个人同意的确切语言的链接或其他访问点，或复现该语言。

items.en: If consent was obtained, were the consenting individuals provided with a mechanism to revoke their consent in the future or for certain uses? If so, please provide a description, as well as a link or other access point to the mechanism (if appropriate).
items.zh: 如果获得了同意，是否向同意的个人提供了未来撤销同意或针对某些用途撤销同意的机制？如果是，请提供描述以及该机制的链接或其他访问点（如适用）。

items.en: Participants were provided with various methods to contact the research team for any questions or concerns (e.g. email, Slack).
items.zh: 参与者被提供了多种联系方式（例如电子邮件、Slack）以联系研究团队提出任何问题或疑虑。

items.en: Has an analysis of the potential impact of the dataset and its use on data subjects (e.g., a data protection impact analysis) been conducted? If so, please provide a description of this analysis, including the outcomes, as well as a link or other access point to any supporting documentation.
items.zh: 是否对数据集及其使用对数据主体的潜在影响进行了分析（例如，数据保护影响分析）？如果是，请描述该分析，包括结果，并提供任何支持文档的链接或其他访问点。

items.en: An impact analysis was conducted to assess the potential impact on the creators of each data instance. Participants engaged in the Review Task were asked to complete a survey measuring their feelings toward the task. The results of this survey demonstrate positive reactions to involvement in the creation of the dataset. For more information on the survey please see §A.2.
items.zh: 进行了影响分析，以评估对每个数据实例创建者的潜在影响。参与审查任务的参与者被要求完成一项调查，衡量他们对任务的感受。调查结果显示，参与者对参与数据集创建持积极反应。有关调查的更多信息，请参见第A.2节。

items.en: Preprocessing / Cleaning / Labeling Was any preprocessing/cleaning/labeling of the data done (e.g., discretization or bucketing, tokenization, part-of-speech tagging, SIFT feature extraction, removal of instances, processing of missing values)? If so, please provide a description. If not, you may skip the remaining questions in this section.
items.zh: 预处理/清洗/标注是否对数据进行了任何预处理/清洗/标注（例如，离散化或分桶、分词、词性标注、SIFT特征提取、移除实例、处理缺失值）？如果是，请提供描述。如果不是，您可以跳过本节中的其余问题。

items.en: Labeling of the data was done by participants in order to tag a subset of the data (see above).
items.zh: 数据标注由参与者完成，以标记数据子集（见上文）。

items.en: Labeling of the data was done with an automated harmlessness classifier (see above).
items.zh: 数据标注使用自动无害分类器完成（见上文）。

items.en: We used a regex filter to remove instances containing PII (see above).
items.zh: 我们使用正则表达式过滤器移除包含个人身份信息（PII）的实例（见上文）。

items.en: Was the “raw” data saved in addition to the preprocessed/cleaned/labeled data (e.g., to support unanticipated future uses)? If so, please provide a link or other access point to the “raw” data.
items.zh: 除了预处理/清洗/标记后的数据外，是否还保存了“原始”数据（例如，以支持未预见的未来用途）？如果是，请提供“原始”数据的链接或其他访问方式。

items.en: Is the software that was used to preprocess/clean/label the data available? If so, please provide a link or other access point.
items.zh: 用于预处理/清洗/标记数据的软件是否可用？如果是，请提供链接或其他访问方式。

items.en: We do not release the harmlessness classifier.
items.zh: 我们不发布无害分类器。

items.en: We provide the regex filter we used to remove PII from the dataset in §A.6.
items.zh: 我们在§A.6中提供了用于从数据集中移除个人身份信息（PII）的正则表达式过滤器。

items.en: Uses Has the dataset been used for any tasks already? If so, please provide a description.
items.zh: 用途：该数据集是否已用于任何任务？如果是，请提供描述。

items.en: Is there a repository that links to any or all papers or systems that use the dataset? If so, please provide a link or other access point.
items.zh: 是否有仓库链接到使用该数据集的任何或所有论文或系统？如果是，请提供链接或其他访问方式。

items.en: What (other) tasks could the dataset be used for?
items.zh: 该数据集还可用于哪些（其他）任务？

items.en: In addition to providing a resource for the research community to further investigate what successful red team attacks look like, this dataset can be used to build (semi-)automated red team techniques and to assess the efficacy of various strategies for mitigating harms in large language models.
items.zh: 除了为研究社区提供资源以进一步研究成功的红队攻击样例外，该数据集还可用于构建（半）自动化红队技术，并评估各种策略在减轻大型语言模型危害方面的有效性。

items.en: Is there anything about the composition of the dataset or the way it was collected and preprocessed/cleaned/labeled that might impact future uses? For example, is there anything that a dataset consumer might need to know to avoid uses that could result in unfair treatment of individuals or groups (e.g., stereotyping, quality of service issues) or other risks or harms (e.g., legal risks, financial harms)? If so, please provide a description. Is there anything a dataset consumer could do to mitigate these risks or harms?
items.zh: 数据集的组成或其收集和预处理/清洗/标记方式是否可能影响未来用途？例如，数据集使用者是否需要了解某些信息，以避免可能导致对个人或群体不公平对待（如刻板印象、服务质量问题）或其他风险或危害（如法律风险、财务损失）的用途？如果是，请提供描述。数据集使用者可以采取哪些措施来减轻这些风险或危害？

items.en: This dataset contains offensive and harmful instances, and should only be used for research purposes and to build the harmlessness classifiers described above. Users of this dataset are advised to engage with the dataset only within the bounds of their personal risk tolerance and practice well-being and resilience exercises when working with this dataset.
items.zh: 该数据集包含攻击性和有害实例，仅应用于研究目的和构建上述无害分类器。建议数据集使用者仅在个人风险承受范围内使用该数据集，并在处理该数据集时进行健康与韧性练习。

items.en: Are there tasks for which the dataset should not be used? If so, please provide a description.
items.zh: 是否存在不应使用该数据集的任务？如果是，请提供描述。

items.en: Just as this dataset can be used to develop safer AI models, it could also be used to train models that produce more harmful responses and should not be used for that purpose. Additionally, the dataset is not comprehensive of all possible harms or red team attacks and should not be treated as such.
items.zh: 正如该数据集可用于开发更安全的AI模型，它也可能被用于训练产生更有害响应的模型，不应为此目的使用。此外，该数据集并不涵盖所有可能的危害或红队攻击，不应被视为如此。

items.en: Distribution Will the dataset be distributed to third parties outside of the entity (e.g., company, institution, organization) on behalf of which the dataset was created? If so, please provide a description.
items.zh: 分发：数据集是否会分发给创建该数据集的实体（如公司、机构、组织）之外的第三方？如果是，请提供描述。

items.en: How will the dataset will be distributed (e.g., tarball on website, API, GitHub)? Does the dataset have a digital object identifier (DOI)?
items.zh: 数据集将如何分发（例如，网站上的tarball、API、GitHub）？数据集是否有数字对象标识符（DOI）？

items.en: Yes. The dataset is publicly available, hosted on GitHub at https://github.com/anthropics/hh-rlhf.
items.zh: 是的。该数据集公开可用，托管在GitHub上，地址为https://github.com/anthropics/hh-rlhf。

items.en: Have any third parties imposed IP-based or other restrictions on the data associated with the instances? If so, please describe these restrictions, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, any relevant licensing terms, as well as any fees associated with these restrictions.
items.zh: 是否有任何第三方对与实例相关的数据施加了基于知识产权或其他方面的限制？如果是，请描述这些限制，并提供链接或其他访问方式，或复制任何相关的许可条款，以及这些限制相关的任何费用。

items.en: Do any export controls or other regulatory restrictions apply to the dataset or to individual instances? If so, please describe these restrictions, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, any supporting documentation.
items.zh: 是否有任何出口管制或其他监管限制适用于数据集或单个实例？如果是，请描述这些限制，并提供链接或其他访问方式，或复制任何支持性文档。

items.en: Maintenance Who will be supporting/hosting/maintaining the dataset?
items.zh: 维护 谁将支持/托管/维护该数据集？

items.en: Anthropic hosts, but does not maintain, the dataset.
items.zh: Anthropic 托管但不维护该数据集。

items.en: How can the owner/curator/manager of the dataset be contacted (e.g., email address)?
items.zh: 如何联系数据集的所有者/管理者/维护者（例如，电子邮件地址）？

items.en: Contact information can be found at https://github.com/anthropics/hh-rlhf.
items.zh: 联系信息可在 https://github.com/anthropics/hh-rlhf 找到。

items.en: Is there an erratum? If so, please provide a link or other access point.
items.zh: 是否有勘误表？如果有，请提供链接或其他访问方式。

items.en: Will the dataset be updated (e.g., to correct labeling errors, add new instances, delete instances)? If so, please describe how often, by whom, and how updates will be communicated to dataset consumers (e.g., mailing list, GitHub)?
items.zh: 数据集是否会更新（例如，纠正标注错误、添加新实例、删除实例）？如果是，请说明更新频率、更新者以及如何将更新通知数据集消费者（例如，邮件列表、GitHub）？

items.en: No. Please contact Anthropic regarding update requests.
items.zh: 不会。如有更新请求，请联系 Anthropic。

items.en: If others want to extend/augment/build on/contribute to the dataset, is there a mechanism for them to do so? f so, please provide a description. Will these contributions be validated/verified? If so, please describe how. If not, why not? Is there a process for communicating/distributing these contributions to dataset consumers? If so, please provide a description.
items.zh: 如果其他人希望扩展/增强/基于/贡献于该数据集，是否有相应的机制？如果有，请提供描述。这些贡献是否会经过验证/核实？如果是，请说明如何验证。如果不是，请说明原因。是否有将这些贡献传达/分发给数据集消费者的流程？如果有，请提供描述。

items.en: Researchers are encouraged to explore and build on the dataset in their own research efforts, but this dataset will remain as-is.
items.zh: 鼓励研究人员在自己的研究工作中探索和基于该数据集进行构建，但该数据集将保持原样。
